bugs.error_message: Rewinding to a just-sent prompt reverts all file changes even though Claude never modified anything
bugs.root_cause: handleRewind always runs restoreCheckpoint(keepTurns+1) which restores the PREVIOUS turn's checkpoint, not the current state. When the target is the last user message with no assistant response, this rolls back to an older snapshot.
bugs.fix: Added undo-send detection at the top of handleRewind: if the rewind target is the last message (user, no assistant response), skip all file operations and just truncate the message + prefill the input. Also handles the edge case where a near-empty assistant response exists.

bugs.error_message: RewindPromptDialog shows the whole path as 'dir' on Windows — path.split('/').pop() returns the entire string when separators are all backslashes
bugs.root_cause: Basename computed via path.split('/').pop() only splits on forward slash. Windows paths use backslashes, so split returns [path] and pop gives the full path, leaving dir = '' and fileName = full path.
bugs.fix: Introduced src/lib/platform.ts helpers (baseName, dirName) that split on both separators, and replaced the inline split with baseName(path) + dirName(path).

bugs.error_message: FileTree produces mixed-separator paths on Windows (e.g. C:\foo/bar/baz.txt) and fails to detect Windows absolute paths from semantic search
bugs.root_cause: All fullPath construction used template-literal '${cwd}/${name}' — appends forward slash even when cwd uses backslashes. Absolute-path detection used relPath.startsWith('/') which misses drive letters (C:\) and UNC (\\server\share).
bugs.fix: Replaced hardcoded slash joins with joinPath(), replaced startsWith('/') with isAbsolutePath() (detects /, \, drive letter, UNC), and replaced .split(/[/\\]/).pop() with baseName().

bugs.error_message: tauriApi.setT64DelegationEnv / clearT64DelegationEnv write .mcp.json with forward-slash paths baked in — MCP args[0] and path strings produce mixed separators on Windows
bugs.root_cause: scriptPath and mcpPath were built with template literals using '/' — on Windows appDir is a backslash path so the resulting script path is C:\Users\x/mcp/t64-server.mjs. Some downstream consumers (Claude CLI, node module resolution) don't normalize and can fail.
bugs.fix: Imported joinPath from platform.ts and replaced the two templated joins in setT64DelegationEnv and clearT64DelegationEnv.

bugs.error_message: navigator.platform is deprecated; no fallback detection on browsers that lock it to a placeholder (e.g. future privacy-reduced webviews)
bugs.root_cause: Each call site did its own navigator.platform.includes('Mac'|'Win') check. That property is deprecated and, when the webview starts returning a reduced value, all three call sites would silently report the wrong platform.
bugs.fix: Centralized detection in src/lib/platform.ts (IS_WIN / IS_MAC) — checks navigator.userAgentData.platform first, falls back to navigator.platform, then to navigator.userAgent. Replaced the three inline checks with the shared constants.

bugs.error_message: ChatInput inline separator detection was fragile — /[\\]/.test(cwd) && !/[/]/.test(cwd) misses mixed-separator cwd values
bugs.root_cause: The prior Windows pass added an ad-hoc absolute-path + separator check. It still failed when cwd contained any forward slash (common after passing through APIs that normalize).
bugs.fix: Replaced the inline regex + sep detection with isAbsolutePath(file) + joinPath(cwd, file) from the centralized platform helper.

bugs.error_message: Windows production build fails or hangs because tauri.conf.json `targets: "all"` tries to build both NSIS and MSI; MSI requires WiX Toolset (~50MB extra dep) which is not installed on the dev machine and not declared in the build prerequisites.
bugs.root_cause: Tauri 2's `targets: "all"` enumerates every bundle format the host platform supports. On Windows that includes NSIS *and* MSI. MSI is generated by WiX, which Tauri downloads on demand only if it's already configured — otherwise the build aborts. CI / fresh dev machines hit this.
bugs.fix: Replaced `targets: "all"` with explicit array `["app", "dmg", "deb", "appimage", "rpm", "nsis"]`. Tauri silently skips formats not applicable to the current host, so macOS still builds app+dmg, Linux still builds deb+appimage+rpm, Windows builds NSIS only — and never tries MSI/WiX.

bugs.error_message: Older WebView2 runtimes on long-lived Windows installs render xterm.js / Monaco / CSS conic-gradient incorrectly, and there is no installer-side enforcement of a minimum.
bugs.root_cause: tauri.conf.json bundle.windows.nsis had no `minimumWebview2Version` — WebView2 is auto-updated by Edge in most cases, but enterprise / locked-down machines can stay on very old (sub-110) builds where modern Web APIs misbehave. Without the gate, the installer happily proceeds and runtime breaks silently.
bugs.fix: Added `nsis.minimumWebview2Version: "110.0.1587.40"` so the NSIS installer triggers a WebView2 update if the local version is older. Also added `nsis.compression: "lzma"` (smaller installer), `webviewInstallMode.silent: true` (no extra UI on first install), and `allowDowngrades: true` (explicit, matches default).

bugs.error_message: resolve_claude_path / resolve_openwolf_path_inner call `where claude.exe` / `where openwolf.exe` — explicit extension disables PATHEXT, so the .cmd shim installed by npm is not found. Then the candidate fallback works in most cases, but users who installed claude via a non-standard PATHEXT-only location get bare `claude` / `openwolf` as the binary name.
bugs.root_cause: Windows `where` respects PATHEXT only when the query has no extension. `where claude.exe` only looks for claude.exe, missing claude.cmd (the npm form).
bugs.fix: Changed the lookup to `where claude` / `where openwolf` (no extension) so PATHEXT finds .cmd shims. First match from `where` output is used.

bugs.error_message: resolve_claude_path() final fallback returns bare string "claude" on all platforms — on Windows Command::new("claude") does NOT apply PATHEXT, so Claude CLI never launches if no candidate path exists.
bugs.root_cause: Unlike the shell, std::process::Command on Windows uses CreateProcessW without PATHEXT expansion. Bare name without extension fails to resolve .cmd shims even when they're on PATH.
bugs.fix: Fallback now returns "claude.cmd" on Windows (parallel to the existing openwolf fallback). Bare "claude" kept for Unix.

bugs.error_message: Five call sites (build_command, ensure_openwolf, and three Haiku subprocess spawners in lib.rs — rewrite_prompt, generate_theme, generate_rewind_summary) call Command::new(&claude_bin)/Command::new(&wolf_bin) directly. Per cerebrum, .cmd/.bat shims on Windows must go through `cmd /C` to get correct arg escaping and console-flash suppression.
bugs.root_cause: Rust 1.77+ does spawn .cmd/.bat via cmd.exe internally, but arg escaping rules differ between direct exe and cmd-dispatched batch files, and PATHEXT resolution for bare names never applies. Uniformly wrapping in cmd /C avoids both pitfalls and keeps CREATE_NO_WINDOW applied in one place.
bugs.fix: Added pub fn shim_command(bin) in claude_manager.rs that returns `cmd /C <bin>` with CREATE_NO_WINDOW on Windows and a plain Command on Unix. Replaced all five Command::new(&bin) sites with claude_manager::shim_command(&bin). Removed redundant creation_flags blocks at call sites.

bugs.error_message: Discord attachment filenames sanitised against control chars and \: * ? " < > | but did not reject Windows reserved device names (CON, PRN, AUX, NUL, COM1-9, LPT1-9) or trailing dots/spaces — a Discord user sending "CON.png" or "file.txt. " causes fs::write to fail silently or collide on Windows.
bugs.root_cause: Windows kernel reserves DOS device names regardless of extension — "CON.png" tries to open the console device. Windows also silently strips trailing dots/spaces, so "file." becomes "file" and "file. " becomes "file", which can collide with sibling files or confuse consumers.
bugs.fix: After the existing separator/control-char filter, trim trailing dots and spaces, then upper-case the stem (pre-first-dot) and compare against the reserved set. Reserved or empty names fall back to literal "file".

bugs.error_message: Rewind does too much AND doesn't fully rewind delegation changes — git checkout HEAD wiped uncommitted work, and only the most recent delegation group's child files were tracked
bugs.root_cause: Two bugs. (A) handleRewind called revertFilesGit on delegation children's modified files; revert_files_git uses `git checkout HEAD -- path` which restores tracked files to the LAST COMMIT — wiping user's pre-session uncommitted edits, other sessions' changes, or parent-session edits not in checkpoints. (B) handleRewind read delState.parentToGroup[sessionId], a single-slot map that each new delegation overwrites (delegationStore L100). If the parent did delegation 1 → completed → delegation 2, rewinding past delegation 1 couldn't find its child modifiedFiles — they were orphaned.
bugs.fix: (A) Replaced revertFilesGit with filterUntrackedFiles → deleteFiles for child files (matches parent handling): only delete untracked/new files; leave tracked-but-modified files alone so user doesn't lose uncommitted work. Log the tracked-left list so user knows. (B) Replaced `parentToGroup[sessionId]` lookup with `Object.values(delState.groups).filter(g => g.parentSessionId === sessionId)` to collect child modifiedFiles from ALL groups ever spawned by this parent, not just the current one. Also end-cancel any still-active groups.

bugs.error_message: Forked chat doesn't respond — sending a prompt in a newly forked session hangs with no streaming
bugs.root_cause: handleFork calls claudeStore.loadFromDisk(newSessionId, forkedMessages), which sets hasBeenStarted=true whenever promptCount>0. actualSend then guarded the fork-first-send path with `if (forkParent && !started)` — so started=true made it fall through to the `--resume <newId>` branch against a session JSONL that the CLI hasn't created yet, causing the CLI to stall silently.
bugs.fix: Reordered branches in actualSend: check `if (forkParent)` first (ignoring `started`). The presence of forkParentSessionId is the unambiguous signal that this is the fork's first send and must use --fork-session. After success the store clears forkParentSessionId so subsequent sends use the normal --resume path.

bugs.error_message: Chat panel background flickers in/out at specific canvas positions; clicking the panel temporarily restores it
bugs.root_cause: `.floating-terminal` uses `overflow: hidden` + `border-radius: 8px` while its parent `.canvas-content` has `transform: scale(z)`. On WebKit, the rounded-corner clip is repainted non-atomically at fractional pixel positions caused by the scale, invalidating the panel's background paint. A click triggers `bringToFront` → zIndex change → repaint, briefly restoring it.
bugs.fix: Promoted `.floating-terminal` to its own compositor layer: added `will-change: transform`, `transform: translateZ(0)`, `backface-visibility: hidden`, `contain: layout paint`, `isolation: isolate`. This keeps the rounded clip stable across subpixel-scaled positions.

bugs.error_message: Voice dictation loses the last word(s) of an utterance; mid-sentence thinking pauses also finalize whisper early
bugs.root_cause: VadAdapter::is_speech returned true only when a single 32 ms Silero sub-window exceeded 0.35 (with a 1-frame hangover). No hysteresis band, no min_silence_duration, no speech_pad. A single dip below threshold inside an utterance (breath, soft consonant tail) started the orchestrator's silence_run counter. If the dip held for 10/25 frames the orchestrator finalized whisper on a buffer that had already cut the trailing audio.
bugs.fix: Replaced VadAdapter with a Silero VADIterator-style state machine: speech_threshold=0.5 activates, silence_threshold=0.35 deactivates (the 0.35-0.5 band is uncertain and leaves counters intact), min_speech_duration_ms=250 gates false starts, min_silence_duration_ms=500 plus speech_pad_ms=300 give ~800 ms of tail before the bool goes false. Tuned voice_manager silence-frame constants down to match (SILENCE_FRAMES_TO_FINALIZE 10->3 for commands, DICT_SILENCE_FRAMES_TO_FINALIZE 25->9 for dictation).

bugs.error_message: Dictation feels laggy and partials stall during long utterances
bugs.root_cause: partial_worker cloned the entire rolling audio buffer every 250ms tick and handed the full thing to whisper. Per-tick cost grew ~O(utterance length) and effectively O(n^2) across a multi-second dictation because each successive tick re-decoded everything from scratch (no_context + no cursor). A 12s utterance re-decoded 12s every tick → 300-700ms decode under Metal → lag stacks, partials stop keeping up with speech, decode queue bloats.
bugs.fix: Replaced full-buffer g.clone() in partial_worker with a trailing window slice g[buf_len - PARTIAL_WINDOW_SECS*SR ..]. Added a 120ms min-new-samples gate so ticks without fresh audio no-op. When the window slides (window_start advances), AgreementBuffer is reset so word-index LCP doesn't correlate across different audio spans. Added a decode watchdog that skips the next tick if a decode exceeds 2x the tick interval. Emits voice-telemetry { decode_ms, window_samples, window_start, buffer_samples, window_shifted, committed_len, tentative_len } per tick so regressions are visible.

bugs.error_message: Reopening Terminal 64 re-executes the last Bash command in an infinite loop — stuck forever
bugs.root_cause: Claude CLI's --resume treats the JSONL as the conversation source-of-truth. If the previous run died mid-tool (app force-quit while a heavy Bash was running), the JSONL contains an assistant tool_use record with no matching user tool_result. On resume, the CLI sees the dangling tool_use and re-executes the tool from scratch. Heavy bash freezes the app (bug-018), user force-quits, dangling tool_use on disk, reopen → retry → freeze → force-quit → replay. Infinite loop.
bugs.fix: Added sanitize_dangling_tool_uses(cwd, session_id): before every cmd.spawn() in spawn_and_stream, scan the session JSONL for tool_use IDs with no matching tool_result block, and append synthetic user records with a cancelled tool_result (is_error: true, content explaining the interruption, preserving parentUuid / cwd / version / gitBranch metadata). This satisfies the CLI's pairing check so --resume skips the dangling calls instead of retrying them.

bugs.error_message: Heavy bash commands run through Claude freeze the entire frontend and app
bugs.root_cause: The Claude CLI stdout reader emits each JSONL line directly as a single Tauri event. A heavy Bash tool_result can be tens to hundreds of MB. Frontend JSON.parse on megabytes of text, React render of the bubble, and Zustand's debounced localStorage persistence (JSON.stringify of the full sessions map) all run on the main thread → renderer freezes for seconds, appearing as a full-app hang.
bugs.fix: Added cap_event_size() wrapper around every emit in spawn_and_stream's stdout reader. If the line exceeds 512KB, parse JSON and rewrite oversized tool_result / text block content to 'head + truncation marker + tail' (96KB + 96KB, UTF-8 char-boundary safe via char_boundary_floor/ceil). If JSON parse fails, fall back to a hard byte-slice cap. The CLI's own JSONL keeps the full output for future turns; only the live UI stream is truncated.

bugs.error_message: After rewinding or forking a Claude session, refreshing/reloading from JSONL shows all the 'deleted' messages back. Users reported rewind+fork as 'hella unreliable'.
bugs.root_cause: Commit db0113b5 (2026-04-15 'lil update') removed the JSONL truncation call from handleRewind, leaving only the in-memory store truncation + --resume-session-at UUID recording for the next send. load_session_history reads every line in the JSONL sequentially (no parentUuid chain walking), so reload shows the original full history. Fork had a parallel bug: fork_session_jsonl copies the full parent JSONL to the new session but never truncates the copy, so the fork displays the entire parent history until a new prompt is sent.
bugs.fix: 1) Re-added the `truncateSessionJsonlByMessages` wrapper in src/lib/tauriApi.ts. 2) In handleRewind (both the undo-send fast path and the main rewind path), call truncateSessionJsonlByMessages after findRewindUuid succeeds — the --resume-session-at UUID is still inside the truncated file. 3) In fork_session_jsonl (Rust), after the std::fs::copy and chain-walk to find the fork UUID, call truncate_session_jsonl_by_messages on the destination so the fork shows only pre-fork messages on reload.

bugs.error_message: On bypass permission mode, Claude silently rejects edits to skills/widgets/MCP/MD files instead of allowing or prompting. Reason written to JSONL: 'Unknown session — denied for safety'.
bugs.root_cause: handle_connection looks up session_id from run_token BEFORE checking the bypassPermissions payload field. When a hook fires with a stale token (race between close_claude_session and an in-flight hook, OR a leftover child process after rewind/spawn), session_id is empty and the PreToolUse branch fail-closes with permissionDecision=deny. The bypass check at the top of the PreToolUse handler never runs for unknown-session cases. Common triggers: rewind (cancel+close unregisters token, new send registers a new token, any residual hook from the old CLI lands on the stale token), skill/widget spawn via spawnClaudeWithPrompt (new session id + timing), permission server port restart.
bugs.fix: Extract permission_mode from the hook payload BEFORE the session lookup gate. On PreToolUse with permission_mode=='bypassPermissions' AND empty session_id, respond with permissionDecision=allow (reason: 'bypassPermissions mode (session not registered)'). The secret in the URL already gates spoofing; an unknown run_token just means 'session unregistered', which shouldn't override user-opted bypass. Non-bypass modes still fail-closed because we need a session_id to route the frontend prompt.

bugs.error_message: In bypass permission mode, edits to Claude Code's hardcoded sensitive-file list (.mcp.json, .claude.json, .zshrc, .git/, .vscode/, .idea/, .claude/, .husky/) silently fail with 'Claude requested permissions to edit X which is a sensitive file.' in --print mode. Claude Code's TUI shows an approval prompt; --print cannot surface one, so the tool errors out and the user sees no actionable UI.
bugs.root_cause: Claude CLI's classifier (RC5/jtH in the minified binary) returns behavior:'ask' with decisionReason.type=='safetyCheck' for these paths. The check runs BEFORE PreToolUse hooks fire (confirmed via probe: no hook event, just an is_error tool_result). --permission-mode bypassPermissions, --dangerously-skip-permissions, and matching permissions.allow rules do not bypass it — the only bypass path is an allow rule whose ruleContent starts with '/.claude/' or '~/.claude/' AND ends with '/**' (YC5=[.git,.vscode,.idea,.claude,.husky]; wC5=[.gitconfig,.gitmodules,.bashrc,.bash_profile,.zshrc,.zprofile,.profile,.ripgreprc,.mcp.json,.claude.json]; allow prefixes lE_='/.claude/**', nE_='~/.claude/**'). No TUI prompt exists in --print mode.
bugs.fix: Layer our own approval flow on top: useClaudeEvents detects tool_result errors with is_error=true and content matching 'which is a sensitive file' OR 'requested permissions to (edit|write to)' for Write/Edit/MultiEdit tools; cached tool_input (new sessionToolInputMaps) plus the file_path are stored as session.pendingSensitiveEdit. ClaudeChat renders a permission-style card reusing .cc-permission styling with 'Allow & Apply' / 'Skip' actions. On Allow, applySensitiveEditAndContinue reads the file via Tauri readFile, applies Write/Edit/MultiEdit semantics locally (replace_all aware, MultiEdit sequential), writes via writeFile, records the path in session.sensitiveEditApprovedPaths, then sends Claude a follow-up user message telling it the edit was manually applied and not to re-issue the tool call. Genericized getSessionMap<V>/evictIfNeeded<V> so the input map can store Record<string, unknown>.

bugs.error_message: After rewinding a Codex session, refreshing from JSONL brings the rewound messages back in the frontend even though Codex no longer sees them in its model context.
bugs.root_cause: Codex app-server rollback prunes the thread context and can persist a thread_rolled_back event, but Terminal 64's load_codex_history_by_thread hydrator only replayed response_item envelopes. It ignored rollback markers, so refresh-from-jsonl reconstructed stale pre-rollback messages that were no longer model-visible.
bugs.fix: Added rollback-aware replay to the Codex JSONL loader. It detects thread_rolled_back/thread_rollback envelopes, drops the requested number of trailing user turns from the hydrated HistoryMessage list, and clears pending tool-call pairing state before continuing through later rollout lines.

bugs.error_message: Codex JSONL refresh can omit edit tool cards for apply_patch edits, so refreshed Codex chats don't match Claude's clickable Edit/MultiEdit rendering.
bugs.root_cause: Codex rollouts persist apply_patch edits as response_item payloads with type custom_tool_call/custom_tool_call_output. load_codex_history_by_thread only materialized function_call/local_shell_call/web_search/mcp_tool_call, so apply_patch history was silently dropped on refresh.
bugs.fix: Added custom_tool_call hydration. apply_patch input is parsed into Edit/MultiEdit-shaped file change metadata with paths and diff snippets, and custom_tool_call_output is paired back to the pending tool call with JSON output unwrapped and exit status respected. The edit preview click path now resolves relative tool paths against the session cwd before reading the file.

bugs.error_message: Codex file-change cards can lose clickable paths/diffs when app-server sends filePath or unified_diff fields, and Codex turn.completed errors may finish silently.
bugs.root_cause: The frontend decoder only accepted path/diff on file_change records, while Codex/app-server variants may use file_path, filePath, unified_diff, or unifiedDiff. Separately, the Codex turn.completed handler ignored parsed.error even though the Rust app-server bridge can attach an error to completed turns.
bugs.fix: Normalized file-change path/diff variants into the provider-neutral tool-call input shape, recorded modified files from all supported path keys, and surfaced completion errors through setError while still running normal turn cleanup.

bugs.error_message: Legacy T64_CODEX_TRANSPORT=exec emits raw rollout-style Codex JSON that the app-server-oriented frontend mostly ignores, so fallback Codex sessions can miss thread IDs, tool cards, context usage, and completion cleanup.
bugs.root_cause: After moving the main Codex path to app-server, the old exec reader still forwarded raw session_meta/event_msg/response_item lines directly. useClaudeEvents expects provider-neutral thread.started/item.started/item.completed/turn.completed shapes, so raw exec events had much lower parity than app-server and Claude.
bugs.fix: Added a Rust-side exec stream translator that converts session_meta to thread.started, token_count to token_usage.updated, task lifecycle events to turn events, and response_item messages/tools to item.completed/item.started pairs. The frontend now preserves tracked tool names and inputs when sparse completed events arrive.

bugs.error_message: Opening the prompt island can lag when a chat has a huge prompt history.
bugs.root_cause: PromptIsland rendered every prompt row on open, reversed the full prompts array, normalized whitespace for every prompt, and formatted every timestamp in one render.
bugs.fix: Bounded initial render cost by showing the latest 120 prompts first, memoizing only the visible slice, and appending older prompts in 120-row batches as the island list is scrolled near the bottom. Added a small older-prompts sentinel row.

bugs.error_message: A first-turn Codex create failure falls through to send_codex_prompt without a Codex thread id, so the backend tries to resume the local Terminal 64 session id as if it were an OpenAI thread.
bugs.root_cause: runProviderTurn used the same create-then-send fallback for fresh Codex sessions and legacy started sessions. On a true first turn there is no external codexThreadId yet, so sendCodexPrompt can only resume the local UUID, which is not a valid Codex thread id.
bugs.fix: Guarded the fallback so fresh Codex sessions throw a visible start error instead of attempting a bogus resume. The old local-id resume fallback remains only for already-started sessions whose metadata predates codexThreadId persistence.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed under exactOptionalPropertyTypes because ProviderTurnInput and ProviderHistoryTruncateInput did not accept explicitly passed undefined values from existing call sites.
bugs.root_cause: The provider runtime contracts used optional properties without `| undefined`, but callers construct object literals with properties like permissionOverride and codexThreadId whose values can be undefined. With exactOptionalPropertyTypes, an explicit undefined value is not assignable to an optional property unless undefined is included in the property type.
bugs.fix: Expanded provider runtime optional input fields to include `undefined`, matching the existing call-site behavior without relaxing tsconfig strictness.

bugs.error_message: Codex file-change paths can be relative, so checkpoint snapshots may read an empty process-relative file and checkpoint restore may later write that raw relative path from the app process working directory.
bugs.root_cause: Codex live/history edit data can store workspace-relative paths. The checkpoint path captured sess.modifiedFiles verbatim, while restore_checkpoint trusted manifest paths with only a substring '..' check. Relative paths therefore crossed from model/workspace semantics into process-relative filesystem writes.
bugs.fix: Resolved checkpoint snapshot paths against the session cwd/effective cwd before readFile/createCheckpoint. Hardened checkpoint directory construction against path-like session ids and made restore_checkpoint reject crafted snapshot filenames, relative restore targets, and restore targets containing parent-directory components.

bugs.error_message: Delegated child sessions did not fully inherit parent provider settings, and the standalone delegation MCP server sent POST requests without Content-Length.
bugs.root_cause: Delegation spawn copied the provider but dropped selected model/effort and forced Codex children to yolo regardless of the parent Codex permission preset. Claude delegation also reused one MCP config labelled Agent for every child. The legacy delegation MCP server wrote JSON bodies using chunked transfer, but the Rust permission server only reads Content-Length bodies.
bugs.fix: Threaded active model/effort/Codex permission into useDelegationSpawn, stamped them onto ephemeral child sessions, sent them in child create requests, created per-Claude-child MCP config files with Agent N labels, and added Content-Length plus HTTP error handling to mcp/delegation-server.mjs.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit failed under exactOptionalPropertyTypes because provider hook call objects explicitly passed possibly-undefined optional fields.
bugs.root_cause: ProviderTurnInput.permissionOverride and ProviderHistoryTruncateInput.codexThreadId are optional properties. With exactOptionalPropertyTypes, assigning a present property whose value can be undefined is not equivalent to omitting that property.
bugs.fix: Built typed provider input objects and only assigned permissionOverride/codexThreadId when the values were not undefined.

bugs.error_message: Chat bottom-stickiness and jump-to-bottom visibility can flicker during streaming even when the user has not scrolled away from the bottom.
bugs.root_cause: ClaudeChat treated every LegendList scroll event as bottom-stickiness intent. Streaming row layout growth can briefly report not-at-end and flip stickyRef false, which stops auto-follow and shows the prompt island/jump button incorrectly. PromptIsland also retained its previous internal scroll position when reopened.
bugs.fix: Moved stickyRef changes to explicit user-intent handlers, throttled scroll telemetry through requestAnimationFrame, added a small streaming-row layout follow pump while sticky, reset the prompt island list to the newest prompts on open, and promoted floating controls to their own compositor layers.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed with src/hooks/useClaudeEvents.ts(152,34): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'ClaudeUsage'.
bugs.root_cause: The current shared worktree has an unresolved type reference in useClaudeEvents.ts, which is outside the chat-scroll files patched by Agent 3 and overlaps the event-decoding lane.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 3; reported in MCP team chat and left for the agent editing useClaudeEvents.ts. Agent 3 verified the frontend bundle with npm run build -- --mode development.

bugs.error_message: Codex live event/rendering parity gaps could make empty events revive spinners, cumulative item text duplicate previews, in-progress file-change deltas look completed, and apply_patch/MCP/local-shell tools render differently after refresh.
bugs.root_cause: The Codex listener treated any parsed event with an empty type as activity, assumed item.text was always a delta, used updateToolResult for patch-only updates, and did not preserve tracked tool identity across output-only completions. The decoder/renderer/history loader also had inconsistent names and path/diff fields for file_change, apply_patch, MCP, and local shell calls.
bugs.fix: Ignored empty Codex event objects, handled cumulative text/output with append-or-replace semantics, added a store helper to patch tool-card input without marking completion, extracted modified files from normalized Codex edit inputs, normalized file-change diff/path fields, and aligned local-shell/MCP/custom tool names between live events and JSONL hydration.

bugs.error_message: A local verification rg command failed with `zsh:1: unmatched "` because the search pattern mixed nested quotes incorrectly.
bugs.root_cause: The shell command used a double-quoted rg pattern containing unescaped double quotes.
bugs.fix: Reran the verification search with a single-quoted pattern and confirmed the intended references.

bugs.error_message: cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings failed with clippy::unnecessary-lazy-evaluations in src-tauri/src/providers/codex.rs for unwrap_or_else(|| json!(null)) fallbacks.
bugs.root_cause: The Codex app-server/exec event translator used lazy closures for cheap null JSON fallbacks. Project CI treats clippy warnings as errors, so these style warnings block the build.
bugs.fix: Replaced the lazy null closures with direct JsonValue::Null fallbacks and reran cargo fmt --check plus cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings successfully.

bugs.error_message: Codex chat displayed `Failed to parse Codex response` mid-stream and virtualized chat rows could visually overlap while tool cards grew.
bugs.root_cause: The shared event size capper could fall back to a raw byte slice for oversized JSON events, producing invalid JSON for the frontend. The Codex listener surfaced that recoverable malformed event as a session error. LegendList rows also used stable message ids even when assistant tool-card output changed row height.
bugs.fix: Made cap_event_size recursively truncate large JSON string fields and always re-serialize valid JSON for parsed events, changed malformed Codex events to be dropped with a console warning instead of a visible session error, and added layout signatures to every assistant row plus finished-tail divider so the virtual list remeasures growing text/tool cards.

bugs.error_message: Consecutive Bash tool calls spammed the chat as separate rows, making assistant messages difficult to read.
bugs.root_cause: The tool-row grouping set only included read/search/fetch tools, so shell command tool calls bypassed the existing collapsed group renderer.
bugs.fix: Added Bash to the groupable tool set and gave Bash groups a compact `Ran N commands` label with command previews in the collapsed header.

bugs.error_message: Refreshing Codex chats from JSONL rendered raw tool names like `exec_command` and `write_stdin`, making the refreshed chat look different from the live stream.
bugs.root_cause: Live app-server command events were normalized to Terminal 64's Claude-like `Bash` shape, but rollout/JSONL hydration preserved raw Codex function-call names. The renderer then used the generic tool-card path instead of the Bash grouping/rendering path.
bugs.fix: Normalized Codex raw shell tool names (`exec_command`, `write_stdin`, `local_shell`, `shell`) into `Bash` and synthesized a `command` field during both Rust history hydration and frontend live decoding.

bugs.error_message: Big workflows with many open terminals/browser panels risk renderer lag and high memory from per-terminal global event listeners, idle browser RAF polling, duplicate resize store writes, and an unbounded JSONL hydration cache.
bugs.root_cause: Every terminal registered its own global Tauri output/exit listener, so each PTY event woke all terminal listeners. Browser panels ran getBoundingClientRect in a requestAnimationFrame loop while idle. Resize updated position and size through separate canvas-store writes. Parsed full-history hydration arrays were cached without a cap.
bugs.fix: Added keyed terminal output/exit dispatchers backed by one Tauri listener, switched XTerminal to per-id subscriptions, replaced browser polling with coalesced canvas/resize-driven sync, added a combined no-op guarded setTerminalFrame path for resize, and capped the hydration cache by least-recently-used entries/message count.

bugs.error_message: CI Rust jobs on macos-latest, ubuntu-latest, and windows-latest failed at `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` on Rust 1.95.0.
bugs.root_cause: GitHub Actions installed stable Rust 1.95.0, which promoted stricter Clippy warnings than the local 1.94.1 toolchain: `unnecessary_sort_by` for descending modified-time sorting and `collapsible_match` for a nested string-length check in the JSON truncation helper.
bugs.fix: Changed the disk session sort to `sort_by_key(|s| std::cmp::Reverse(s.modified))` and collapsed the JSON string branch into a match guard. Verified with `cargo +stable fmt --check`, `cargo +stable clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo +stable check --all-targets` on Rust 1.95.0.

bugs.error_message: Dragging files or images onto the canvas could attach them to the last active chat, and in some layouts appeared to affect multiple chats.
bugs.root_cause: Tauri drag/drop events are window-level, but ClaudeChat only let the active session handle drops. That made file targeting depend on focus history instead of the cursor position, while each mounted chat still had its own listener and drag-over state.
bugs.fix: Marked chat roots with data-session-id and routed drag enter/over/drop to the chat under the cursor, falling back to the nearest visible chat rectangle. The chosen chat is activated before files attach, and .zip files still stay out of the chat path for widget import handling.

bugs.error_message: Codex MCP servers showed as configured instead of connected, and the built-in T64 MCP could appear twice in the MCP dropdown.
bugs.root_cause: The Codex app-server emits MCP startup changes as `mcpServer/startupStatus/updated` with status `ready`, but Terminal 64 did not translate that notification into the live MCP store. The dropdown also chose either live status or configured servers wholesale and treated `t64`, `terminal-64`, and `codex/terminal-64` as separate rows.
bugs.fix: Translated Codex startup status notifications to provider events, normalized `ready` to `connected`, merged live MCP status over configured entries, and canonicalized T64 MCP aliases into one built-in dropdown row.

bugs.error_message: Many open chat and terminal panels still did repeated renderer work on window drag/drop and unrelated canvas-store updates.
bugs.root_cause: Each ClaudeChat mounted its own Tauri drag/drop listener and recomputed chat hit-testing for every drag event. Canvas and XTerminal subscribed to the whole canvas store, so terminal movement, z-index, and other unrelated writes still rewrote canvas transform styles or ran xterm zoom/fit checks.
bugs.fix: Moved chat file drag/drop routing behind one module-level listener with per-chat registration, then guarded Canvas and XTerminal subscriptions so they only run when pan/zoom or zoom actually changes.

bugs.error_message: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` failed with `clippy::items-after-test-module` after adding tests to `src-tauri/src/providers/util.rs`.
bugs.root_cause: The new `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block was inserted before later production items (`session_jsonl_path` and `sanitize_dangling_tool_uses`). The repo treats Clippy warnings as errors, so test modules must come after all non-test items in the module.
bugs.fix: Moved the provider utility test module to the end of the file and ran `cargo fmt`.

bugs.error_message: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` failed while Agent 4 had in-progress edits in `src-tauri/src/permission_server.rs`: duplicate `PendingMap` plus `PendingPermission` field mismatches.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel backend lifecycle edits temporarily left two incompatible `PendingMap` type aliases in the permission server while code expected the newer `PendingPermission` wrapper.
bugs.fix: Agent 5 did not modify the permission server; reported the blocker in team chat and left the in-progress backend fix to Agent 4.

bugs.error_message: Backend session/resource lifecycle could leave stale PTY/provider map entries, temp permission files, pending permission waiters, and widget manifest cache entries after panel/session close or fast process exit.
bugs.file: src-tauri/src/pty_manager.rs, src-tauri/src/providers/claude.rs, src-tauri/src/providers/codex.rs, src-tauri/src/permission_server.rs, src-tauri/src/widget_server.rs, src-tauri/src/lib.rs
bugs.root_cause: PTY instances were only removed by explicit close, so shells that exited naturally stayed in the manager map. Claude/Codex close delegated to cancel, which killed the child but kept the instance slot until reader cleanup. Permission pending requests were keyed only by request id, so unregistering a session could not release blocked hook/MCP threads. Claude sessions with an empty frontend id registered temp hook files against an empty id before the backend generated the real id. Widget manifest cache entries were not invalidated when widget folders were deleted or replaced.
bugs.fix: Inserted PTY instances before starting readers and made reader EOF remove only the matching generation; made Claude/Codex close remove and wait the child directly; tracked pending permission requests by session id and denied them on unregister; pre-generated empty Claude session ids before permission registration and unregistered temp files on spawn failure; added widget manifest cache invalidation on widget delete/install.

bugs.error_message: Widget/browser panels could leak background work or native webviews during iframe reloads, duplicate subscriptions, or rapid panel close/reopen.
bugs.root_cause: Widget hot-reload polling ran every 1.5s even while hidden, allowed overlapping mtime IPCs, and forced a first-poll iframe reload. Plugin subscriptions with the same id overwrote old handles without closing them, voice and terminal-ready listeners could resolve after bridge teardown, and async native browser creation could complete after React unmount. BrowserManager also attempted duplicate child-webview creation without first removing stale ids.
bugs.fix: Baselined widget mtimes, paused polling while hidden, guarded polling with an in-flight flag, cancelled async widget server setup, replaced the embedded-browser idle RAF loop with event-coalesced bounds sync, closed duplicate plugin subscriptions, cancelled late voice/terminal-ready listeners, closed embedded/native browsers if create resolves after unmount, and made BrowserManager replace duplicate ids plus prune stale visibility entries.

bugs.error_message: Frontend persistence paths could block the renderer and grow localStorage with many open workflows.
bugs.root_cause: Canvas used a permanent 5-second interval and rewrote unchanged layout snapshots; Claude metadata reparsed and rewrote the full localStorage key on every debounced save while retaining stale unnamed/delegation rows; delegation persistence wrote full group payloads, including large result/action/context strings and old completed groups.
bugs.fix: Replaced canvas polling with dirty-triggered scheduled saves plus serialized-payload dedupe; added cached Claude metadata reads, idle debounced writes, stale unnamed/delegation cleanup, entry caps, and compact quota retry; added idle delegation saves with stale completed-group pruning and bounded persisted result/action/context strings while keeping in-memory runtime state unchanged.

bugs.error_message: npm run build failed while parallel ClaudeChat extractions were in progress: duplicate declarations for handleAttach, handlePasteImage, and hasPlan in src/components/claude/ClaudeChat.tsx.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent extraction patches left legacy in-file attachment and plan declarations alongside newly extracted hook declarations.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 3 because the duplicate declarations are owned by Agent 2 attachment extraction and Agent 4 plan extraction; reported in t64 team chat. Verified ChatEditOverlay.tsx independently with tsc.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit failed while parallel ClaudeChat extractions were in progress: ChatMessageList callback prop mismatches, ClaudeChat session type mismatch from stripped streamingText, scroll handler signature mismatches, and missing setPlanContent references.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent message-list and plan-mode extraction patches have integration type mismatches outside Agent 1's provider controls write scope.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 1; ProviderControls.tsx produced no TypeScript diagnostics and the failure was reported in t64 team chat for the owning agents.

bugs.error_message: npm exec tsc -- --noEmit failed after Agent 2 attachment extraction: ChatMessageList callback/LegendList prop types and useChatPlanMode ClaudeSession type expectations still fail in the shared ClaudeChat extraction branch.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent message-list and plan-mode extraction patches left type mismatches outside the attachment/drop hook scope. The new useChatAttachments hook produced no TypeScript diagnostics.
bugs.fix: Agent 2 kept write scopes disjoint and reported the failure in t64 team chat; after the owning agents landed their fixes, npm exec tsc -- --noEmit and npm run build both passed for the shared tree.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit reported Agent 5 list extraction type errors: ChatMessageList onRewind/onFork callback signatures, LegendList ListFooterComponent footer type, and useChatRows requiring ClaudeSession with streamingText.
bugs.root_cause: The extracted list component used approximate callback/footer prop types, and the row hook depended on the full ClaudeSession type even though ClaudeChat's shallow selector intentionally omits streamingText.
bugs.fix: Changed ChatMessageList props to match ChatMessage callback signatures and LegendList footer expectations, and narrowed useChatRows to the minimal session fields it reads. A remaining useChatPlanMode session type error is owned by the plan-mode extraction.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed after plan-mode extraction: useChatPlanMode expected ClaudeSession but ClaudeChat passes a shallow session object with streamingText omitted.
bugs.root_cause: The extracted hook typed its session parameter as the full ClaudeSession even though it only needs messages, isStreaming, and planModeActive. ClaudeChat intentionally strips streamingText to avoid per-token rerenders.
bugs.fix: Narrowed useChatPlanMode to a local ChatPlanSession interface containing only the fields used by plan detection/completion logic. npm run typecheck now passes.

bugs.error_message: Codex fork opened a Claude session with the Codex transcript JSONL and failed on send.
bugs.root_cause: The fork flow added the new canvas panel, then awaited provider-specific fork preparation before creating the child session in claudeStore. ClaudeChat could mount during that await and create the session with the default Anthropic provider.
bugs.fix: Seed the child claudeStore session immediately after addClaudeTerminalAt with the parent provider, cwd, model, effort, and Codex permission before any async fork work runs.

bugs.error_message: Claude rewind failed after truncation with stderr: No message found with message.uuid of: 44768e6e-2d52-4d86-bcf9-014070618437.
bugs.root_cause: Terminal 64 physically rewrites the Claude JSONL during rewind, but still stored and reused a resumeAtUuid through --resume-session-at. Claude's CLI can reject that UUID after the file rewrite even though the truncated JSONL is the desired source of truth.
bugs.fix: Stop sending --resume-session-at for Claude rewind resumes, clear any legacy resumeAtUuid after truncation and on the next provider turn, and let normal session resume use the already-truncated JSONL.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit failed: src/lib/codexEventDecoder.ts(297,35): Argument of type 'ProviderToolInput | CodexItem' is not assignable to parameter of type 'ProviderToolInput'.
bugs.root_cause: A parallel decoder edit currently passes a union containing CodexItem to a helper that requires ProviderToolInput.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 1; outside provider manifest ownership. Logged so the decoder owner can resolve the union narrowing.

bugs.error_message: cargo fmt --check failed after adding provider command registry wiring.
bugs.root_cause: New import lists and a registry helper signature were not in rustfmt's preferred layout.
bugs.fix: Ran cargo fmt from src-tauri to apply canonical formatting.

bugs.error_message: python -m json.tool .wolf/buglog.json failed: zsh:1: command not found: python.
bugs.root_cause: This shell exposes python3 but not python.
bugs.fix: Validated the JSON with python3 -m json.tool instead.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit failed: src/lib/codexEventDecoder.ts(297,35): Argument of type 'ProviderToolInput | CodexItem' is not assignable to parameter of type 'ProviderToolInput'.
bugs.root_cause: The Codex changed-path helper accepted a union of raw CodexItem and normalized ProviderToolInput, and TypeScript could not narrow the record-shaped ProviderToolInput branch safely.
bugs.fix: Split the helper into codexItemChangedPaths(item) and codexInputChangedPaths(input), then updated useClaudeEvents to call the normalized-input helper for patched tool inputs.

bugs.error_message: Provider metadata was duplicated across flat ClaudeSession fields, making future provider-specific metadata widen the shared session shape.
bugs.root_cause: Codex-specific thread, permission, and fork transcript metadata lived beside provider-neutral session fields and persistence wrote only the flat shape.
bugs.fix: Added canonical providerState, normalized legacy flat mirrors through updateSession, bumped persisted metadata schema to v5, and migrated old flat metadata on load.

bugs.error_message: Legacy or hot-reloaded in-memory Codex sessions without providerState would hydrate and route as Anthropic.
bugs.root_cause: After introducing canonical providerState, hydrateFromJsonl and selectSessionProvider read providerState directly without falling back to the legacy flat provider/codexThreadId mirrors. A live HMR session created before the migration can still lack providerState.
bugs.fix: Added fallback from providerState to legacy provider and codexThreadId fields during provider history hydration and provider selection.

bugs.error_message: User sent 'yo' to Codex GPT-5.5, then queued repeated 'y' prompts; Codex showed 'Turn never started' and 'Codex app-server exited without output'.
bugs.root_cause: Terminal 64 only marked a Codex chat streaming after provider events arrived, so fast follow-up sends were treated as new turns and each new app-server spawn killed the previous worker before it reached turn/start. The Codex 0.125 app-server contract also requires thread/start/resume booleans and turn text_elements that Terminal 64 omitted, making startup more fragile before a thread id was returned.
bugs.fix: Set session streaming immediately when a provider send begins so extra prompts queue instead of replacing the worker; added required experimentalRawEvents=false and persistExtendedHistory=true to app-server thread params; added text_elements=[] and effort to turn/start params.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed with missing names in src/hooks/useDelegationOrchestrator.ts and missing ProviderCreateRequest/ProviderSendRequest exports in src/lib/tauriApi.ts.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent provider runtime/IPC refactors removed or renamed direct IPC imports and generic provider IPC types while dependent files still referenced the old names.
bugs.fix: Reported to the team because the failures were in files owned by concurrent Agent 1/Agent 2 work; after those concurrent provider runtime/IPC edits landed, npm run typecheck passed.

bugs.error_message: Isolated npx tsc check for src/lib/providerModularity.verification.ts failed with Property 'hot' does not exist on type 'ImportMeta'.
bugs.root_cause: The one-off tsc command did not load Vite client ambient types, while claudeStore references import.meta.hot.
bugs.fix: Re-ran the isolated verification check with --types vite/client; the focused providerModularity.verification.ts compile check passed.

bugs.error_message: npm run build -- --noEmit failed with CACError: Unknown option `--noEmit`
bugs.root_cause: The project has a dedicated `typecheck` script for `tsc --noEmit`; passing --noEmit through Vite build is not supported.
bugs.fix: Used `npm run typecheck` for TypeScript no-emit verification and `npm run build` for the production frontend build.

bugs.error_message: cargo fmt --check failed after adding provider IPC helper functions
bugs.root_cause: Manual Rust edits did not match rustfmt line wrapping for match arms and chained method calls.
bugs.fix: Ran `cargo fmt`, then re-ran `cargo fmt --check` successfully.

bugs.error_message: Codex app-server startup/turn failures could hang or be hidden, and rapid second sends could kill an active app-server turn.
bugs.root_cause: The app-server worker used blocking stdout reads with no startup deadline, marked turn_started immediately after writing turn/start instead of after an app-server response or notification, and removed/killed any existing instance for the same local session before spawning the next app-server process.
bugs.fix: Added app-server startup and turn-start timeouts, JSON-RPC method-specific error messages, initialize protocol diagnostics, stronger thread id extraction, confirmed-turn tracking, and active-turn rejection for app-server transport while leaving legacy exec transport replacement behavior unchanged.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed in ClaudeChat.tsx with missing provider/runtime names after concurrent frontend refactors.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent provider routing changes removed or renamed direct Codex/Claude imports while remaining ClaudeChat call sites still referenced decodeCodexPermission, sendCodexPrompt, createCodexSession, sendClaudePrompt, history loaders, mapHistoryMessages, and ChatMessageData.
bugs.fix: Reported the blocker in team chat; after concurrent frontend integration settled, reran npm run typecheck successfully.

bugs.error_message: Integration verification initially ran cargo fmt --check from the repo root and jq '.[-3:]' against an object-shaped buglog.
bugs.root_cause: Terminal 64's Rust crate lives under src-tauri, and .wolf/buglog.json stores entries under a top-level bugs array rather than as the root array.
bugs.fix: Reran cargo fmt --check from src-tauri, inspected buglog structure directly, and recorded the command-shape gotcha for future sessions.

bugs.error_message: Codex sessions could surface Claude-native slash commands and compact controls, and auto-compact still used Claude-only send IPC.
bugs.root_cause: Compact and native slash-command behavior was keyed off hardcoded Claude UI assumptions instead of provider manifest capabilities; the auto-compact callback called sendClaudePrompt directly.
bugs.fix: Added provider manifest capabilities for native slash commands and compact, filtered Claude built-ins for unsupported providers while keeping skills, gated compact UI/status rows/hints, and routed auto-compact through runProviderTurn.

bugs.error_message: cargo fmt --check failed after adding provider_history_* Rust commands.
bugs.root_cause: New generic provider history command implementation had rustfmt drift in chained call wrapping.
bugs.fix: Ran cargo fmt from src-tauri and re-ran cargo fmt --check successfully.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed with TS2740: partial providerState fixture was missing full ClaudeSession fields after delegation child runtime extraction.
bugs.root_cause: The new helper typed parentSession as ClaudeSession even though it only needs provider metadata and skipOpenwolf; the verification fixture correctly passed a minimal metadata source.
bugs.fix: Narrow the helper input type to the exact metadata fields consumed instead of requiring a full store session.

bugs.error_message: Verification command `node mcp/delegation-server.mjs; test $? -eq 1` exited 1 because the current agent process inherited active T64_DELEGATION_* environment variables.
bugs.root_cause: The negative-path verification assumed delegation env vars were absent, but the Terminal 64 MCP session itself exports them.
bugs.fix: Reran the negative-path check with T64_DELEGATION_PORT, T64_DELEGATION_SECRET, T64_GROUP_ID, and T64_AGENT_LABEL explicitly unset.

bugs.error_message: First-turn send with no working directory can leave the chat stuck streaming.
bugs.root_cause: useChatSend set isStreaming=true before validating the first-turn working directory. The no-CWD guard then returned after setting an error without clearing streaming state.
bugs.fix: Moved the first-turn working-directory validation before setStreaming(true), so the validation error exits without marking the session busy.

bugs.error_message: Backend delegation MCP config writers used forward-slash string joins, and delegation HTTP routes accepted malformed or oversized bodies into the team-chat store.
bugs.root_cause: create_mcp_config_file, ensure_t64_mcp, and ensure_codex_mcp built t64-server.mjs and .mcp.json paths with format!("{}/..."), which can produce mixed separators on Windows. The permission server also capped Content-Length with min(1MB), causing oversized requests to be read as truncated JSON, and malformed delegation JSON fell back to Value::default(), allowing blank group messages.
bugs.fix: Added a native Path::join helper for backend MCP script paths, joined .mcp.json via Path, reject over-1MB bodies with 413, reject malformed delegation JSON and missing group_id with 400, cap retained/read delegation messages, and sanitize the MCP read_team last parameter.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed during parallel provider audit work: ClaudeChat queued prompt payload uses possibly undefined legacy text, and useDelegationOrchestrator references missing lifecycle completion export plus implicit any parameters.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel in-progress edits changed queued prompt and lifecycle completion APIs while Agent 2 was verifying refresh-from-history. The failing symbols are outside the authoritative refresh path.
bugs.fix: Reported exact failures in team chat; parallel owners completed their queue/lifecycle fixes, and Agent 2 reran npm run typecheck successfully.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck initially failed during lifecycle bus verification with ClaudeChat.tsx string | undefined queue errors from parallel work; rerun passed after concurrent edits settled.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel queued prompt payload edits temporarily left ClaudeChat with an undefined legacy text value at typecheck time while Agent 3 was verifying lifecycle bus changes.
bugs.fix: Reported the failure in team chat, avoided touching the queue-owned file, and reran npm run typecheck after parallel edits settled; the command passed.

bugs.error_message: Rich queued prompt payloads preserved normal prompts but special commands still bypassed the queue while a turn was streaming.
bugs.root_cause: ClaudeChat handled /loop, /reload-plugins, /delegate, and skill slash commands before the general streaming queue check, so those paths could still call actualSend during an active provider turn and lose transformed provider prompt metadata.
bugs.fix: Added queued command metadata kinds for loop/reload/delegate-plan/skill; queued each special command's display text and transformed provider prompt when streaming; replay now restores delegate and loop side effects before sending.

bugs.error_message: Terminal 64 still lagging heavily; user suspects a widget is memory leaking and needs per-widget diagnostics to debug it.
bugs.root_cause: Widget panels had no host-side visibility into reload churn, bridge traffic, retained plugin/bus/voice/terminal subscriptions, embedded browser state, or optional widget self-reported metrics, making leaks hard to isolate.
bugs.fix: Added a low-overhead widget metrics store, instrumented WidgetPanel with batched bridge traffic and resource counters, added t64:debug-metrics self-report support, and surfaced active/tracked widget diagnostics plus console snapshot logging in Settings.

bugs.error_message: Widget diagnostics showed every active widget receiving about 9k outbound bridge messages in 3 minutes, while the app still had a visible lag spike about every 5 seconds.
bugs.root_cause: WidgetPanel default-pushed live AI session updates, including token-level streaming text, into every iframe whether the widget asked for session events or not. The bundled project-intel widget also polled OpenWolf daemon status on a fixed 5000ms interval.
bugs.fix: Made AI session bridge events opt-in via t64:subscribe-session-events, throttled streaming-text delivery for subscribers, tracked session-event subscriptions in widget diagnostics, documented the opt-in API, and changed project-intel daemon polling to a non-overlapping visibility-aware 30s timeout loop.

bugs.error_message: After widget bridge traffic dropped to normal levels, Terminal 64 still had periodic lag spikes, pointing at another fixed-cadence main-thread task.
bugs.root_cause: Canvas session persistence used a fixed 5000ms throttle from the first dirty mark. If layout stayed dirty during ongoing interaction, the renderer could synchronously stringify and write canvas session state to localStorage every 5 seconds.
bugs.fix: Replaced fixed 5s throttle with a 1s quiet-time debounce followed by requestIdleCallback when available, added HMR idle cleanup, and logs saveSession duration/byte count when canvas persistence itself is slow.

bugs.error_message: User still sees lag after widget bridge and canvas autosave fixes; installed ro-sync widget has a direct 5000ms health loop not visible in Terminal 64 bridge counters.
bugs.root_cause: The previous diagnostics only covered Terminal 64 bridge traffic and known persistence paths. Custom widget code can still run direct timers/fetches inside iframe contexts, and ro-sync specifically used setInterval(healthTick, 5000) against its local daemon.
bugs.fix: Added a global renderer performance monitor for event-loop lag, browser long tasks, and slow storage writes, exposed it in Settings, and changed the installed ro-sync health loop to a non-overlapping visibility-aware 30s timeout loop.

bugs.error_message: User still sees lag but Settings -> Performance says 0 events tracked.
bugs.root_cause: The first performance monitor only recorded event-loop drift above 120ms, Long Task API entries, and slow storage writes. Visible compositor/iframe/render hitches can drop frames without producing those events.
bugs.fix: Added requestAnimationFrame frame-drop detection at 55ms gaps, made frame drops show as warnings in Settings, and added a persisted Pause Widgets toggle that unmounts all widget iframes and closes embedded browser views for isolation.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed: src/components/widget/WidgetPanel.tsx(1127,53): error TS1382: Unexpected token. Did you mean {'>'} or >?
bugs.root_cause: JSX text in the paused-widget placeholder contained a raw '->' sequence; the greater-than character was parsed as JSX syntax.
bugs.fix: Escaped the arrow as {'->'} in the JSX text and reran npm run typecheck successfully.

bugs.error_message: User clicked the diagnostics Log button but had no accessible console inside the app.
bugs.root_cause: The Widget Debug and Performance Log buttons only wrote console.table output, which is not visible to normal Terminal 64 users without devtools.
bugs.fix: Changed both diagnostic Log actions into Copy actions that still log internally but also write JSON snapshots to the clipboard and show a temporary Copied state.

bugs.error_message: User confirmed the lag stops when widgets are paused.
bugs.root_cause: The global widget pause isolated the frame drops to widget iframe/compositor work, but there was no way to pause/resume individual widgets to identify the exact offender.
bugs.fix: Added persisted per-widget paused ids, per-card Pause/Resume controls in Widget Debug, and made WidgetPanel treat either global pause or per-widget pause as an iframe unmount/embedded-browser close condition.

bugs.error_message: User identified the ro-sync widget as the remaining lag source after per-widget pause isolation.
bugs.root_cause: ro-sync's active view opened a daemon WebSocket and synchronously appended a DOM log row, trimmed the log, updated counters, updated unsynced UI, and forced scroll on every op frame. After DOM batching, lag persisted because both the active stream and app stream still JSON-parsed every high-volume op frame before ignoring or sampling it.
bugs.fix: Patched the installed widget so active log rendering is RAF-batched, pending log frames are capped and collapsed, scroll is only forced when already near the bottom, op/stat badge writes are batched, hidden documents drop log work, app-level op preview buffering is bounded, and daemonWS supports raw-frame skipping before JSON.parse. The app stream now counts op bursts without parsing payloads, and the active stream samples 20 parsed op frames per second while collapsing the rest. User still reports lag after unpausing, so the durable fix is tracked as widget render-process isolation rather than further per-widget throttling.

bugs.error_message: Agent 4 briefly introduced invalid JSX/TypeScript syntax while adding widget render-mode fields to the Widget Debug snapshot map.
bugs.root_cause: The callback was accidentally written as an object-returning arrow function while declaring local const values inside the returned object literal.
bugs.fix: Changed the map callback to a block body that computes requested/effective render mode and then returns the snapshot object; npm run typecheck passes.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit failed with missing imports/types in src/components/widget/WidgetPanel.tsx during parallel widget bridge extraction.
bugs.root_cause: A concurrent bridge extraction left WidgetPanel referencing symbols such as PluginStreamHandle, WidgetSessionEventName, buildStateSnapshot, useClaudeStore, and bridge API helpers without local imports or definitions.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 3 because WidgetPanel is owned by the parallel Agent 1 extraction; Agent 3 reported the failure in team chat and avoided the file to prevent conflicts.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed in src/components/widget/useWidgetBridgeHost.ts: startVoice/stopVoice imported from tauriApi and OpenDialogOptions included filters: undefined.
bugs.root_cause: The extracted widget bridge host imported voice capture helpers from the wrong module and passed an explicitly undefined optional filters field while exactOptionalPropertyTypes is enabled.
bugs.fix: Removed the stale tauriApi voice imports and built the file-picker options object so filters is omitted unless present. Full typecheck remains blocked by the separate WidgetPanel extraction issue in bug-088.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit failed with missing imports/types in src/components/widget/WidgetPanel.tsx during parallel widget bridge extraction.
bugs.root_cause: The extraction was temporarily split between WidgetPanel and the new host hook, leaving bridge-owned symbols referenced from the panel while the hook file was incomplete.
bugs.fix: Moved the bridge command switch, session event fanout, resource subscriptions, and provider-turn helpers into useWidgetBridgeHost, reduced WidgetPanel to iframe transport wiring, and verified npm run typecheck passes.

bugs.error_message: Native widget webview mode rendered at the wrong size/scale on the zoomable canvas and ro-sync still lagged when unpaused.
bugs.root_cause: Tauri child webviews are positioned outside the React DOM and do not inherit CSS transforms from the canvas, so bounds-only syncing is insufficient. ro-sync also kept realtime daemon streams open in its widget UI, making the visible widget surface itself the expensive component.
bugs.fix: Added native widget webview zoom syncing from canvas zoom, exposed set_widget_webview_zoom, and patched the installed ro-sync widget so app-level realtime daemon streaming is disabled by default and the Activity live log starts paused behind a manual Start live log button.

bugs.error_message: ro-sync still lagged Terminal 64 when unpaused even after realtime UI streams were throttled/disabled.
bugs.root_cause: The host widget hot-reload poll called widget_file_modified every 1.5s for each mounted widget and recursively scanned the whole widget folder. The installed ro-sync widget folder was 5.5G with 42,295 files, including daemon/target and tools trees, so the app repeatedly walked huge build/tool directories while the widget was mounted.
bugs.fix: Changed widget_file_modified to skip heavy dependency/build/tool directories such as target, tools, node_modules, Packages, dist, build, coverage, vendor, and hidden directories. For the installed ro-sync folder this reduces the hot-reload scan from 42,295 files to 54 files.

bugs.error_message: Codex app-server turn/start failed code=-32602: Input exceeds the maximum length of 1048576 characters when forking a Codex session.
bugs.root_cause: The Codex fork fallback seeded the next first turn with the full visible transcript and raw tool input JSON, which can exceed app-server's turn/start input limit on large sessions.
bugs.fix: Added a hard 900k character Codex prompt budget, bounded latest-first transcript rendering, per-message/tool input/result caps, truncation markers, and a verification fixture for oversized fork seeds.

bugs.error_message: Claude session UI reopened empty and refresh-from-jsonl stayed empty after app restart, while Claude itself still remembered the previous work.
bugs.root_cause: Claude history hydration only looked at the JSONL path derived from Terminal 64's stored cwd, and createSession adopted a late cwd for an existing row without persisting that metadata. If cwd metadata was missing/stale or normalized differently than Claude's project directory, the UI could miss the JSONL even though --resume still worked by session id.
bugs.fix: Made read-only Claude history hydration/stat/metadata/tail resolve an existing JSONL by session id across ~/.claude/projects when the exact cwd-derived path is missing, and persisted cwd immediately when createSession adopts it for an existing non-ephemeral session.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed after adding the Codex oversized seed fixture.
bugs.root_cause: The transcript loop indexed an array under noUncheckedIndexedAccess, and the verification fixture explicitly set toolCalls: undefined while exactOptionalPropertyTypes is enabled.
bugs.fix: Guarded the indexed transcript message before rendering and built fixture ChatMessage objects without explicit undefined optional fields.

bugs.error_message: Codex forking needed to use the upstream app-server fork contract instead of falling back to transcript seeding.
bugs.root_cause: Terminal 64's native Codex fork request omitted the required persistExtendedHistory flag and only attempted thread-id based fork. If app-server could not resolve the thread id, the frontend fell back to seeded transcript forking, which is less correct and can hit turn/start input limits.
bugs.fix: Updated Codex fork to send persistExtendedHistory, parse several app-server thread id result shapes, and retry thread/fork with the local rollout JSONL path found under ~/.codex/sessions before allowing the frontend seed-transcript fallback.

bugs.error_message: Error: Session ID 40862248-a9d4-437a-b0cb-e87021c7a78a is already in use when prompting an empty-looking previously used Claude session.
bugs.root_cause: The frontend could mark a recovered Claude session as not started when its visible message list was empty, so the provider create path spawned Claude with --session-id for an id that already had a transcript. The CLI reports that asynchronously on stderr after providerCreate has already returned, so the runtime catch fallback to --resume never ran.
bugs.fix: Added a shared Claude transcript resolver that locates an existing JSONL by session id across ~/.claude/projects. Claude create_session now uses --resume instead of --session-id whenever that transcript already exists, and dangling-tool sanitization uses the same resolver.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed during provider IPC contract refactor with concurrent errors in src/components/ui/BrandLogos.tsx and src/lib/providerModularity.verification.ts.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent edits introduced exactOptionalPropertyTypes and ProviderUiMetadata fixture mismatches outside the Agent 1 provider IPC contract files.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 1; reported in team chat for the owning agents while confirming no providerIpc/runtime/tauriApi type errors surfaced before these blockers.

bugs.error_message: cargo fmt --check failed after refactoring backend provider command request dispatch.
bugs.root_cause: New provider command helper functions and registry dispatch calls were not in rustfmt's canonical multiline layout.
bugs.fix: Ran cargo fmt from src-tauri, then reran cargo fmt --check, cargo check, and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings successfully.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed after adding future-provider modularity verification coverage.
bugs.root_cause: The first fixture placed @ts-expect-error before object declarations while TypeScript reported the error on the provider property, the stub manifest missed newly added ProviderUiMetadata fields, and the fixture still used unparameterized ProviderCreateRequest/ProviderSendRequest after provider IPC moved to mapped request contracts.
bugs.fix: Moved @ts-expect-error comments onto the exact discriminant properties, reused a Codex request for the current fail-closed IPC assertion, added brandTitle and emptyStateLabel to the stub manifest, and switched IPC checks to ProviderCreateRequest<"openai"|"anthropic"> / ProviderSendRequest<"openai">.

bugs.error_message: src/components/ui/BrandLogos.tsx failed npm run typecheck under exactOptionalPropertyTypes after adding ProviderLogo.
bugs.root_cause: ProviderLogo forwarded className={className} into AnthropicLogo/OpenAILogo while LogoProps declared className?: string, which does not accept an explicitly passed undefined value when exactOptionalPropertyTypes is enabled.
bugs.fix: Changed LogoProps.className to className?: string | undefined and reran npm run typecheck successfully.

bugs.error_message: cargo fmt --check failed after adding provider history capability fields and unsupported history IPC responses.
bugs.root_cause: New Rust imports and provider history helper changes were not in rustfmt's canonical multiline layout.
bugs.fix: Ran cargo fmt from src-tauri, then reran cargo fmt --check, cargo check, and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings successfully.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed during the parallel providerMetadata migration because delegation fixtures still referenced providerState.openai.
bugs.root_cause: ProviderSessionState moved OpenAI-specific session data under providerMetadata.openai, while the delegation helper and verification fixture still read the removed providerState.openai field.
bugs.fix: Changed delegation child runtime inheritance and verification assertions to use getOpenAiProviderSessionMetadata(), and changed fixture providerState rows to providerMetadata.openai.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit failed during parallel provider refactors with ClaudeChat permission-control type errors.
bugs.root_cause: The permission-control refactor briefly left ClaudeChat using stale permission constants and prop names while Agent 2's metadata migration was already typechecking the combined worktree.
bugs.fix: Agent 3 completed the permission helper integration and updated ClaudeChat/useChatSend call sites; a follow-up npx tsc --noEmit run passed.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed because useClaudeEvents.ts still read providerState.openai after ProviderSessionState moved OpenAI metadata under providerMetadata.
bugs.root_cause: The auto-compact provider turn path was added in the event hook while the provider metadata migration removed the direct providerState.openai field.
bugs.fix: Imported getOpenAiProviderSessionMetadata() and used it to populate Codex thread id and selected permission fallback for the auto-compact provider turn.

bugs.error_message: cargo check failed with duplicate Tauri command macro symbols: __cmd__load_session_history, __cmd__stat_session_jsonl, __cmd__truncate_session_jsonl_by_messages, __cmd__fork_session_jsonl, and __cmd__delete_session_jsonl.
bugs.root_cause: Provider history adapters needed to call Claude JSONL helpers, but making existing #[tauri::command] handlers pub(crate) caused Tauri to emit duplicate command macro symbols.
bugs.fix: Restored private Tauri command handlers and added separate pub(crate) *_impl helpers for provider adapter calls; cargo check and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passed.

bugs.error_message: Manual `rustfmt --check src-tauri/src/providers/registry.rs src-tauri/src/providers/events.rs` failed with `async fn is not permitted in Rust 2015`.
bugs.root_cause: Direct rustfmt invocations outside Cargo default to Rust 2015 unless an edition is supplied, while these provider modules use async functions from the Rust 2021 crate.
bugs.fix: Re-ran the targeted formatter check with `rustfmt --edition 2021 --check ...`, then formatted the touched provider files with `rustfmt --edition 2021 ...`; the targeted check passed.

bugs.error_message: Provider scaffold generator needed follow-up fixes after the first draft would generate `Opencode*` symbols and duplicate `ProviderKind::OpenCode` in the checklist for an OpenCode scaffold.
bugs.root_cause: The initial generator derived code symbols only from the lowercase provider id and always emitted a new ProviderKind enum variant snippet, ignoring the human label and existing OpenCode backend variant.
bugs.fix: Added a PascalCase `--symbol`/label-derived symbol prefix, used it for generated TypeScript/Rust identifiers, and made the backend ProviderKind checklist conditional when the requested kind already exists as OpenCode.

bugs.error_message: Initial apply_patch attempts for provider-neutral claudeStore/buglog updates failed because expected context no longer matched the active parallel worktree.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel edits shifted the surrounding store and OpenWolf buglog context, so broad multi-hunk patches used stale trailing lines.
bugs.fix: Read the current file context and applied smaller scoped patches for storage key/type/store aliases plus this buglog entry; npm run typecheck passed after the code changes.

bugs.error_message: `npm run tauri dev` failed while linking with `ld: library 'clang_rt.osx' not found` and a stale `/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/usr/lib/clang/15.0.0/lib/darwin` search path.
bugs.root_cause: The macOS Command Line Tools on this machine provide Clang 21, but Rust's linker invocation still included an old Clang 15 runtime search path, so `-lclang_rt.osx` could not resolve even though the current CLT runtime existed.
bugs.fix: Updated the Tauri build script to query the active compiler resource directory and add its `lib/darwin` directory as a native Rust link-search path when `libclang_rt.osx.a` is present.

bugs.error_message: Claude rewind should work like t3code rollback instead of physically truncating JSONL and then resuming without a valid branch cursor.
bugs.root_cause: The Claude provider history path rewrote the session JSONL and returned no resume cursor, while the frontend runtime also failed to pass `resume_session_at` to Claude sends. Refreshing from JSONL could then replay future/orphaned messages instead of the active branch.
bugs.fix: Changed non-empty Claude rewind to return the active `--resume-session-at` UUID without rewriting JSONL, passed that cursor on the next send, and made Claude history hydration parse the active parentUuid chain or the pending rewind cursor so orphaned future turns stay hidden.

bugs.error_message: After an empty Claude rewind, the chat wiped fully empty and follow-up sends failed with `No conversation found with session ID`.
bugs.root_cause: The `keep_messages=0` fallback still physically truncated Claude JSONL into a file with no visible conversation, while the frontend could keep treating the session as already started and send `--resume` against that invalid file.
bugs.fix: Changed empty Claude rewind to delete the session JSONL, made zero-prompt sessions route through fresh create, made truncateFromMessage clear hasBeenStarted when promptCount reaches zero, and made Claude create ignore existing JSONL files that contain no visible conversation records.

bugs.error_message: Vite Fast Refresh invalidated `ChatMessage.tsx` because its `GROUPABLE_TOOLS` export was incompatible with consistent component exports.
bugs.root_cause: A React component module exported a non-component Set used by row construction, which violates vite-plugin-react's Fast Refresh export expectations.
bugs.fix: Moved `GROUPABLE_TOOLS` into a small `toolGrouping.ts` helper module and updated chat message/row imports.

bugs.error_message: Claude rewind failed with `No transcript leaf found in JSONL` after a normal short chat.
bugs.root_cause: Claude JSONL can contain attachment and hook records after assistant messages and between transcript messages. The rewind cursor lookup counted every record's parentUuid as a child, so a Stop-hook attachment made the latest assistant look non-leaf, while attachment-skipping chain parsing could also lose valid transcript ancestors.
