bugs.fix: Changed rewind cursor and active-history parsing to choose leaves using only user/assistant transcript references, while still recording all UUID parent links so chain traversal can pass through Claude attachment nodes.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed with `src/components/claude/ClaudeChat.tsx(1528,77): ... Property 'streamingText' is missing ... but required in type 'ProviderSession'.`
bugs.root_cause: A ProviderSession-shaped object literal in the empty-chat provider picker UI path omits the required streamingText field.
bugs.fix: Reported to the team because ClaudeChat.tsx was owned by Agent 2 for the UI patch; a subsequent combined `npm run typecheck` passed after the UI shape was corrected.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed with `src/components/claude/ClaudeChat.tsx(1528,77): ... Property 'streamingText' is missing ... but required in type 'ProviderSession'.`
bugs.root_cause: ProviderChat's shallow selector intentionally omits streamingText from the session object to avoid whole-chat rerenders during token streaming, but the new provider-picker eligibility helper required a full ProviderSession.
bugs.fix: Changed the helper to accept the render-time session view (`Omit<ProviderSession, "streamingText">`) and kept streaming status as the separate boolean it already consumed.

bugs.error_message: npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false failed with `src/stores/claudeStore.ts(1170,13): error TS2451: Cannot redeclare block-scoped variable 'providerState'.` and the same error at line 1188.
bugs.root_cause: The pre-start provider switch guard introduced a `providerState` constant, then the same block declared another `providerState` for the replacement defaults.
bugs.fix: Renamed the replacement default state variable to `nextProviderState` and passed it as `providerState: nextProviderState`.

bugs.error_message: Widget bridge `t64:create-session` always defaulted invalid/missing providers to Anthropic and created provider-locked sessions, so no-prompt/no-provider widget requests could not open an unlocked empty chat.
bugs.root_cause: The bridge resolved `provider` with `isProviderId(rawProvider) ? rawProvider : "anthropic"` and passed a locked session creation path regardless of whether the widget supplied a prompt or explicit provider.
bugs.fix: Added bridge-specific provider resolution: explicit provider payloads lock to that provider, prompt-only requests inherit the active provider or fall back to Anthropic and lock, and blank requests create an Anthropic-initialized but provider-unlocked empty chat.

bugs.error_message: Codex stayed in plan collaboration mode after the user clicked build-after-plan actions.
bugs.root_cause: Plan build actions relied on natural-language text like "Plan mode is over" instead of passing Codex app-server `collaboration_mode: "default"` through the provider turn payload.
bugs.fix: Extended the chat send options path to carry explicit Codex collaboration mode for build-after-plan actions, set queued plan-build prompts to `default`, and added a runtime request-shaping regression assertion.

bugs.error_message: npm install @tauri-apps/plugin-updater @tauri-apps/plugin-process --package-lock-only failed with `getaddrinfo ENOTFOUND registry.npmjs.org`.
bugs.root_cause: The sandbox has no DNS/network access to the npm registry, so npm could not fetch metadata for newly added frontend plugin packages.
bugs.fix: Manually added the package.json dependencies and lockfile entries using published package metadata; local `npm run typecheck` still cannot resolve the modules until dependencies are installed with network access.

bugs.error_message: npm run tauri signer generate -- --no-password -w /tmp/terminal64-updater.key failed with `unexpected argument '--no-password'`.
bugs.root_cause: The installed Tauri CLI signer subcommand uses `--ci` to skip prompts; it does not support a `--no-password` flag.
bugs.fix: Generated the updater keypair with `npm run tauri signer generate -- --ci -w /tmp/terminal64-updater.key` and used the generated public key in tauri.conf.json.

bugs.error_message: cargo check --all-targets failed while updating crates.io index with `Could not resolve host: index.crates.io`.
bugs.root_cause: The sandbox has no DNS/network access to crates.io, and the new Tauri updater/process crates are not present in the local Cargo lock/cache.
bugs.fix: Recorded the verification blocker; cargo fmt passed, but cargo check must be rerun in an environment that can fetch crates and update Cargo.lock.

bugs.error_message: Cursor provider showed thinking but no assistant output because headless cursor-agent exited at the workspace trust prompt.
bugs.root_cause: The new Cursor adapter launched `cursor-agent -p --output-format stream-json` without `--trust`. In headless print mode Cursor exits with `Workspace Trust Required` unless `--trust`, `--yolo`, or `--force` is passed.
bugs.fix: Added `--trust` and `--approve-mcps` to the Cursor CLI command builder, mapped Terminal 64 plan mode to Cursor `--mode plan`, and logged no-stdout Cursor exits before emitting the provider error event.

bugs.error_message: Fast provider turns could show thinking and then finish with no assistant message.
bugs.root_cause: On `turn_completed`, the provider event handler called `flushBeforeFinalization()` but then read `session.streamingText` from the stale Zustand state object captured before the flush. Cursor emits assistant text and result back-to-back, so the freshly flushed streaming text was cleared instead of finalized.
bugs.fix: After flushing pending text, read a fresh store snapshot before finalizing assistant text. Also made the Cursor decoder render the final `result` text when no assistant text event was observed.

bugs.error_message: Cursor agents did not get Terminal 64 MCP/delegation tools reliably.
bugs.root_cause: Cursor runs MCP servers from `.cursor/mcp.json` and expects the Cursor-owned MCP process to receive delegation env through that config. `/delegate` also depended on a fragile assistant-formatted text block. A later smoke test showed that setting content-length response framing was wrong for Cursor CLI and made the server fail to load.
bugs.fix: Updated Cursor MCP config to use `${env:...}` delegation env placeholders, added the `StartDelegation` MCP tool for parent agents, made the Cursor adapter run `cursor-agent mcp enable terminal-64` before turns, changed `/delegate` to ask the agent to call that tool instead of writing a block, added a tagged JSON fallback, and later migrated Cursor MCP response framing back to newline in bug-127.

bugs.error_message: User reported that chat disappears after app reload because there is no chat persistence.
bugs.root_cause: Cursor sessions do not yet hydrate from provider-owned history like Claude JSONL or Codex rollout files, and the shared store previously persisted only lightweight metadata plus seed transcripts. Normal user/assistant/tool message mutations therefore were not written to localStorage for Cursor.
bugs.fix: Added bounded `localTranscript` persistence for Cursor sessions, hydrated it during session creation, saved metadata after Cursor user/assistant/tool/truncate mutations, and added a typechecked provider verification fixture for Cursor transcript hydrate/writeback.

bugs.error_message: User reported Cursor MCPs still were not loading.
bugs.root_cause: Terminal 64 wrote `.cursor/mcp.json` with `T64_MCP_OUTPUT_FRAMING=content-length`. Cursor CLI spawned the MCP server, but hung during handshake and eventually reported `terminal-64: Error: Connection failed`. Direct server smoke tests hid this because they used content-length framing intentionally. Separately, Cursor default mode rejects MCP tool calls unless the turn runs with `--force`.
bugs.fix: Changed Cursor MCP config generation to `T64_MCP_OUTPUT_FRAMING=newline`, made the Cursor runtime call `ensureCursorMcp` on every turn so existing bad configs migrate, kept `StartDelegation` exposed as an MCP tool, marked read-only tools with annotations, and forced only the Cursor `/delegate` orchestrator turn through `bypass_all` so the MCP call can execute.

bugs.error_message: Cursor successfully called terminal-64-StartDelegation, but Terminal 64 did not spawn the delegation team chat.
bugs.root_cause: Cursor emits MCP tool calls with provider-prefixed names like `terminal-64-StartDelegation` and nests the real tool payload under `tool_call.mcpToolCall.args.args`. The frontend delegation detector only matched an exact normalized `StartDelegation` name and parsed top-level `context`/`tasks`, so it ignored the successful tool call.
bugs.fix: Updated the Cursor event decoder to unwrap nested `args.args` into provider tool input, updated the delegation detector to accept provider-prefixed names ending in `StartDelegation`, and added a provider event normalization fixture for the Cursor MCP shape.

bugs.error_message: User reported Cursor sessions did not show MCP status like the other providers.
bugs.root_cause: Cursor's stream-json turn output does not provide Claude/Codex-style MCP startup status events. Terminal 64 had configured and approved the MCP server, but no provider-neutral `mcp_status` event was emitted for the UI to render.
bugs.fix: Added synthetic Cursor `mcp_status` provider events after MCP approval, including ready/error state, stdio transport, and the expected tool list for parent vs delegation-child sessions. Updated the Cursor decoder and provider verification fixture to normalize that event.

bugs.error_message: A provider-session-store import rewrite in App.tsx left an extra closing import line, producing invalid TypeScript syntax.
bugs.root_cause: The patch changed the provider store import path by adding a new `} from` line instead of replacing the existing line.
bugs.fix: Replaced the full import terminator with `} from "./stores/providerSessionStore";` so the import block is syntactically valid.

bugs.error_message: `npx tsc --noEmit` failed with provider permission/control and provider modularity verification type errors during parallel agent work.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent provider-permission edits introduced temporarily inconsistent source discriminants, providerPermissions requirements, and manifest controls requirements while Agent 1 was verifying naming-entrypoint changes.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 1; reported the exact failing files and ownership to the team so the permission-control owner can finish the in-flight migration.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed during Agent 6 history-source work with the same in-flight provider permission/control and provider modularity verification errors.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel provider-permission/control edits temporarily require `providerPermissions` and manifest `controls` while some verification fixtures and permission-control comparisons still use the older shape.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 6; errors are owned by the concurrent permission-control migration. Agent 6 verified the failure set did not point to the new history-source fields directly.

bugs.error_message: `cargo fmt --check` failed during Agent 7 backend adapter verification because `src-tauri/src/lib.rs` had unformatted new `ensure_*_mcp_impl` wrapper signatures.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel backend lifecycle edits added long single-line function signatures after Agent 7 had intentionally avoided modifying `src-tauri/src/lib.rs`.
bugs.fix: Agent 3 ran cargo fmt after completing the backend lifecycle helper edits; cargo fmt --check now passes.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed during Agent 5 event-semantics verification with in-flight provider permission/control and provider modularity verification errors.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel provider-permission/control and delegation verification edits temporarily disagree on providerPermissions, manifest controls, and parseDelegationStartFromMessage fixture shapes while Agent 5 was verifying event semantics.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 5; the failing files are owned by concurrent permission/delegation work. The failure output did not reference src/lib/providerEventSemantics.ts or src/hooks/useClaudeEvents.ts.

bugs.error_message: Agent 7 ran an `rg` shell command with unescaped Markdown backticks, causing zsh to try executing `ProviderAdapter`.
bugs.root_cause: The search pattern was wrapped in double quotes, so shell command substitution interpreted the backticked term before `rg` ran.
bugs.fix: Reran the search with the pattern in single quotes so backticks were treated literally.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed during Agent 4 delegation workflow extraction with provider modularity fixture shape errors and in-flight providerPermissions patch errors.
bugs.root_cause: The new delegation workflow fixtures passed full ChatMessage objects to a helper typed as a content/toolCalls pick, while concurrent provider-control edits made the future-provider fixture require manifest controls and providerPermissions.
bugs.fix: Trimmed delegation parser fixtures to the helper input shape, added controls/providerPermissions to the future-provider verification stub, and kept the concurrent store providerPermissions patch shape aligned. `npm run typecheck` now passes.

bugs.error_message: `npm run build -- --noEmit` failed with Vite `Unknown option --noEmit` during Agent 2 verification.
bugs.root_cause: `npm run build` invokes `vite build`, not the TypeScript compiler, so forwarding `--noEmit` passes an unsupported flag to Vite.
bugs.fix: Ran the correct verification command, `npx tsc --noEmit`, which passed after the provider permission/control migration.

bugs.error_message: `rg` failed with `the literal "\n" is not allowed in a regex` during Agent 2 cleanup search.
bugs.root_cause: A multiline regex was passed to ripgrep without `--multiline`; ripgrep rejects literal newline matching by default.
bugs.fix: Split the cleanup search into two single-line ripgrep commands, which returned no remaining non-compatibility matches.

bugs.error_message: `git status --short --ignored=.wolf/hooks/_session.json .wolf/token-ledger.json` failed with `fatal: Invalid ignored mode '.wolf/hooks/_session.json'`.
bugs.root_cause: `git status --ignored` accepts ignored-mode values such as traditional/matching/no, not file paths. I passed a path as the option value while trying to inspect OpenWolf runtime-state files.
bugs.fix: Dropped the invalid status form and continued verification with normal `git status --short`, `git diff --stat`, and targeted file scans.

bugs.error_message: `rg -n "... Backend `ProviderAdapter` ..." .wolf/cerebrum.md` caused zsh command substitution and printed `zsh:1: command not found: ProviderAdapter`.
bugs.root_cause: I repeated the known shell gotcha from bug-135 by putting a search pattern containing Markdown backticks inside double quotes.
bugs.fix: Reran the ripgrep search with the pattern in single quotes so backticks were treated literally.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` failed during Agent 6 verification with ProviderControlsMetadata/selectedControls/providerRuntime generic errors from concurrent provider modularity edits.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel provider-control/runtime work left the worktree in an intermediate state while Agent 6 was verifying event semantics; the TypeScript failures did not reference providerEventSemantic* files or useProviderEvents.
bugs.fix: No Agent 6 code fix was needed; recorded the transient verification blocker and continued with scoped error filtering.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed during Agent 1 verification with ProviderControlsMetadata.model/effort, selectedControls fixture/store, futureUnsupported.history, and later setProviderControl store errors from concurrent provider modularity edits.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel provider-control/runtime/store work left the shared worktree in an intermediate TypeScript state after the provider-neutral rename. The failures were not references to removed Claude implementation paths; the old-path scan only found intended compatibility shims.
bugs.fix: No Agent 1 rename fix was applied; reported the scoped failures to the team and left ownership to the agents changing ProviderControls, selectedControls, and runtime fixtures.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed with ProviderControlsMetadata.model/effort errors during parallel provider control migration
bugs.root_cause: Parallel Agent 4 changed ProviderControlsMetadata from fixed model/effort properties to manifest-declared control arrays while ProviderChat/ProviderControls still read legacy fixed slots.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 2; scope-owned ProviderTurnInput/providerOptions type errors were resolved, remaining control metadata errors belong to Agent 4 in-flight work.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` failed during Agent 3 verification with ProviderControlsMetadata.model/effort errors and missing ProviderSessionStoreState.setProviderControl implementation.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel provider-control migration left fixed-slot ProviderControlsMetadata reads and a store interface/action mismatch in the shared worktree while Agent 3 verified the OpenAI permission migration.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 3; scoped permission checks passed and the remaining selectedControls/control metadata errors were reported to the team for Agent 4 ownership.

bugs.error_message: `cargo check` failed with unresolved imports `providers::ProviderCommandLifecycle`, `providers::ProviderOpenWolfOptions`, and `providers::ProviderPreparedCommand`.
bugs.root_cause: New provider lifecycle preparation structs were added in providers::traits and imported through the providers module facade before that facade re-exported them.
bugs.fix: Re-exported ProviderCommandLifecycle, ProviderOpenWolfOptions, and ProviderPreparedCommand from src-tauri/src/providers/mod.rs; reran cargo check successfully.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` failed with ProviderControlsMetadata.model/effort and ProviderControlsProps selectedModel/selectedEffort errors.
bugs.root_cause: Parallel provider-control migration removed fixed model/effort control metadata while ProviderChat and ProviderControls still had legacy fixed-control reads during Agent 5 verification.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Agent 5; this is the same shared Agent 4-owned control migration blocker reported by bugs 142-144, and Agent 5 scoped verification passed for Rust lifecycle changes.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` failed with `ProviderSession | undefined` not assignable to `DelegationParentSessionSource | undefined` because selectedControls used the wrong map shape.
bugs.root_cause: DelegationParentSessionSource modeled selectedControls as a single provider's flat ProviderControlValueMap, but ProviderSession carries the full provider-keyed ProviderSelectedControlsMap.
bugs.fix: Changed DelegationParentSessionSource.selectedControls to ProviderSelectedControlsMap while keeping DelegationChildRuntimeSettings.selectedControls as the flat selected control map for the resolved child provider.

bugs.error_message: `get_delegation_messages` returned loaded delegation messages without `group_id`, while the shared `DelegationMsg` type and live `delegation-message` event include `group_id`.
bugs.root_cause: The Tauri command manually rebuilt each message as JSON and omitted `group_id`, creating a shape mismatch between initial SharedChat loads and live events.
bugs.fix: Included `group_id` in the `get_delegation_messages` JSON response so persisted/loaded team chat data matches the Rust event payload and frontend type.

bugs.error_message: The Tauri window opened but the React frontend rendered blank after delegation/provider refactors.
bugs.root_cause: The delegation store loaded `terminal64-delegations` during module import and trusted the parsed localStorage shape before building task/session indexes. Stale or malformed persisted groups could throw before React mounted.
bugs.fix: Added persisted delegation group/task normalization before pruning and index construction, dropping invalid rows and defaulting missing fields so bad localStorage can no longer blank the frontend at startup.

bugs.error_message: React rendered an empty root with `Maximum update depth exceeded` when replaying the user's saved provider panels.
bugs.root_cause: A Zustand selector returned `resolveSessionProviderState(...)`, which constructs a new provider-state object on every `getSnapshot`. Saved provider panels mounted `ProviderChat`, React detected the uncached snapshot, then entered an update loop and unmounted the root.
bugs.fix: Changed ProviderChat to select stable persisted control values instead of a freshly-created provider-state object, and replaced fresh `[]`/`{}` fallback selectors with stable constants.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed during parallel provider refactors with removed `codexThreadId` history fields still referenced and an exactOptionalPropertyTypes `skipOpenwolf` object-literal mismatch in `src/lib/tauriApi.ts`.
bugs.root_cause: In-flight cross-agent edits had already changed provider history/launch contracts, but dependent call sites were not all updated when Agent 1 ran full TypeScript verification.
bugs.fix: Coordination blocker logged; Agent 1 left these files to the owning agents and will rerun verification after their scopes settle.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed during parallel provider/settings refactors with `SettingsPanel.tsx` forwarding `title: string | undefined` to an exact optional prop and `useDelegationOrchestrator.ts` referencing `getOpenAiProviderSessionMetadata` without an in-scope import.
bugs.root_cause: In-flight cross-agent edits changed settings UI props and provider-history cleanup logic while full TypeScript verification was rerun from Agent 1.
bugs.fix: Coordination blocker logged; Agent 1 left the files to the owning agents and will rerun verification after those scopes settle.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with `src/hooks/useDelegationOrchestrator.ts(138,28): error TS2304: Cannot find name 'getOpenAiProviderSessionMetadata'.`
bugs.root_cause: The history-cleanup refactor removed the OpenAI metadata import entirely, but the same file still needs that helper for provider turn thread IDs outside the history contract path.
bugs.fix: Restored the `getOpenAiProviderSessionMetadata` import for send-path thread IDs while keeping `deleteProviderHistory` inputs provider-neutral.

bugs.error_message: Delegation child provider turns always included `permissionOverride: "bypass_all"`, so providers whose manifest child runtime policy was `permissionPreset: "selected"` still ran with bypass/yolo instead of the selected provider permission.
bugs.root_cause: The child turn input builder hard-coded the Claude-shaped bypass override rather than deriving permission fields from `ProviderManifest.delegation.childRuntime.permissionPreset`.
bugs.fix: Added `resolveDelegationChildRuntimePermission()`: `bypass_all` policies set a provider bypass id and `permissionOverride: "bypass_all"`, while `selected` policies keep the selected provider permission and omit the override. Verification fixtures now assert OpenAI/Cursor selected requests do not force bypass and Anthropic child requests do.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with `src/lib/providerModularity.verification.ts(237,46): error TS2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'color' does not exist in type 'ProviderControlOption'.`
bugs.root_cause: A parallel provider manifest verification edit added a `color` field to a `ProviderControlOption` fixture while that type does not currently include `color`.
bugs.fix: Coordination blocker logged; Agent 2 left the fixture to the provider manifest owner and continued verifying its own provider-history changes separately.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with `src/lib/providerModularity.verification.ts(242,46): error TS2353: Object literal may only specify known properties, and 'color' does not exist in type 'ProviderControlOption'.`
bugs.root_cause: The future-provider permission fixture inlined a permission option inside the generic `ProviderControlMetadata.options` context, so TypeScript applied excess-property checks against `ProviderControlOption` instead of `PermissionOption`.
bugs.fix: Moved the fixture permission option into a named `PermissionOption[]` constant and referenced that from the control options; `npm run typecheck` then passed.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with `src/lib/tauriApi.ts(1089,51): Type '{ ... skipOpenwolf: boolean | undefined; }' is not assignable to type 'SpawnProviderTurnInputArgs' with exactOptionalPropertyTypes.`
bugs.root_cause: The prompt-spawn helper call forwarded `skipOpenwolf: skip` even when `skip` was undefined, violating this repo's exact optional property rule.
bugs.fix: Changed the call to spread `skipOpenwolf` only when the option is defined, so undefined optional fields are omitted.

bugs.error_message: `spawnProviderSessionWithPrompt` used global `settingsStore.claudePermMode` and passed a Claude-shaped `permissionMode` into non-Claude provider turns.
bugs.root_cause: The spawn helper still had legacy Claude settings plumbing after provider sessions became provider-neutral, so seeded OpenAI/Cursor prompts could receive Anthropic permission semantics instead of provider-owned default permissions.
bugs.fix: Factored `buildSpawnProviderTurnInput()` to seed controls from provider manifests, derive `providerPermissionId` via `getDefaultProviderPermissionId()`, and only add `permissionMode` when the provider is Anthropic. Added provider modularity fixtures for OpenAI and Cursor prompt spawns.

bugs.error_message: `ProviderSessionDialog` exposed provider chips but `App.tsx` ignored the selected provider and pre-created every new dialog session as Anthropic.
bugs.root_cause: The dialog callback named the selected provider parameter `_provider` and always passed `"anthropic"` to `createSession`, so the new-session provider selection was only cosmetic until the empty-chat picker changed it.
bugs.fix: Changed the callback to pass the selected provider through to `createSession`, while keeping the session provider-unlocked so the empty-chat picker can still switch before first send.

bugs.error_message: Cursor delegation children did not get MCP tools required to send to team, read team chat, or report done.
bugs.root_cause: Cursor Agent headless MCP did not expand `${env:...}` placeholders from `.cursor/mcp.json`, so even child turns launched with `T64_DELEGATION_*` env still loaded the standalone `StartDelegation` tool set.
bugs.fix: Cursor MCP setup now writes actual delegation env values into `.cursor/mcp.json` for active child turns, rewrites back to standalone env for normal turns, and passes the same env into `cursor-agent mcp enable` before spawn. Added Rust regression tests for delegated and standalone Cursor MCP config generation.

bugs.error_message: After refreshing the app, every provider session defaulted back to Anthropic.
bugs.root_cause: `ProviderChat` mounted saved sessions by calling `createSession` with the current default available provider, and `createSession` preferred that caller default over persisted provider metadata.
bugs.fix: Changed session creation to prefer persisted `providerState.provider`/flat provider metadata over caller defaults whenever saved metadata exists, while keeping caller provider defaults for brand-new sessions. Added provider modularity regression coverage for OpenAI and Cursor refresh hydration.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with exactOptionalPropertyTypes on runtimeToolResult: patch?: ProviderToolPatch | undefined was passed to buildProviderToolResult.
bugs.root_cause: runtimePatch(value.patch) was called directly inside a conditional object spread, so TypeScript could not narrow the second call away from undefined under exact optional property types.
bugs.fix: Stored runtimePatch(value.patch) in a local const before spreading, so the patch property is omitted unless a concrete ProviderToolPatch exists.

bugs.error_message: `cargo fmt --check` failed after adding Cursor runtime envelope helpers because rustfmt wanted multiline formatting in cursor_tool_entry.
bugs.root_cause: The new helper chain was not rustfmt-formatted.
bugs.fix: Ran targeted rustfmt with --edition 2021 on the Rust files touched by the Events task.

bugs.error_message: npm run typecheck failed while Metadata was wiring provider runtime metadata. Remaining errors: src/lib/providerSnapshots.ts references missing values/placement fields on snapshot option/control descriptors.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent provider snapshot/option descriptor work left providerSnapshots.ts out of sync with the descriptor types while Metadata changes typechecked past their own strict-shape errors.
bugs.fix: Metadata fixed its own runtime metadata typing and fixture errors; providerSnapshots.ts remains owned by the Snapshot/Options agents.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` failed after snapshot IPC work with missing `providerSnapshots` helper exports and stale descriptor fields.
bugs.root_cause: The initial snapshot helper only exposed the backend loader while parallel Picker UI already imported display/control helper APIs, and parallel Options work changed descriptors from placement/values to scope/options.
bugs.fix: Added snapshot display/control/permission/capability helper exports and aligned frontend plus backend snapshot descriptors with kind/scope/options. Reran npm typecheck successfully.

bugs.error_message: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` failed because the new registry snapshot test used `expect()`.
bugs.root_cause: The registry snapshot conformance test used Option::expect for missing snapshots, but this repository denies clippy::expect_used even in tests.
bugs.fix: Replaced expect() with explicit match branches that panic with a targeted message only on missing required snapshots. Reran clippy successfully.

bugs.error_message: `cargo test providers::snapshots providers::registry -- --nocapture` failed with `unexpected argument 'providers::registry'`.
bugs.root_cause: Cargo accepts only one test name filter before the `--` test harness arguments; passing two module filters is a command invocation error.
bugs.fix: Reran focused tests with a single module filter, which covered the intended tests successfully.

bugs.error_message: `cargo test providers::registry -- --nocapture` failed with E0061: `emit_provider_runtime_event` in `src/providers/cursor.rs:557` was called with 4 arguments but the helper in `src/providers/mod.rs` takes 2.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent provider event-contract edits updated the shared runtime-event helper signature while Cursor emission code still used the previous provider/session/value argument shape.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by the Snapshots agent because the failing code belongs to the EventContract task; reported the blocker in team chat and left snapshot ownership changes unchanged.

bugs.error_message: `cargo test providers::snapshots -- --nocapture` and `cargo test providers::registry -- --nocapture` failed with E0061: `emit_cursor_runtime_events` in `src/providers/cursor.rs` takes 2 arguments but calls at lines 600, 637, and 758 supplied 3.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent provider event-contract edits changed the Cursor runtime-event helper signature while some call sites still passed an explicit session id.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by the Capabilities agent because the failing code belongs to the EventContract task; reported the blocker in team chat and continued with capability-only static checks.

bugs.error_message: `cargo test providers::events -- --nocapture` failed with E0061 because Cursor runtime-event helper calls still supplied the removed session_id argument.
bugs.root_cause: The EventContract helper refactor changed `emit_cursor_runtime_events` to take only the AppHandle and `Vec<ProviderRuntimeEvent>`, but three remaining call sites still used the old `(handle, session_id, events)` signature.
bugs.fix: Removed the stale session_id argument at the MCP status, synthesized error, and synthesized empty-result call sites, then reran rustfmt.

bugs.error_message: ProviderRuntimeEvent/provider-event suppression was provider-scoped, so one migrated session could drop legacy events for other active sessions on the same provider.
bugs.root_cause: `subscribeProviderEventIngestion` tracked providers that had emitted on the shared `provider-event` topic in a `Set<ProviderId>`, then suppressed all legacy Claude/OpenAI events for that provider instead of only suppressing duplicate events for the same provider session.
bugs.fix: Refactored ingestion through a pure router keyed by `(provider, sessionId)`, kept legacy events flowing for other sessions/providers, decoded canonical `ProviderRuntimeEvent` envelopes generically with Cursor preserving its decoder path, and added mixed Cursor/Claude/OpenAI frontend fixtures.

bugs.error_message: `cargo check` failed with E0063/E0308 in `src/providers/snapshots.rs`: `ProviderSnapshotOptionValue` initializers are missing `value`, and `default_value` now expects `ProviderSnapshotControlValue` instead of `String`.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent control-kind schema edits changed the Rust snapshot wire types while snapshot builder initializers had not yet been updated to the new field/type shape.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by EventContract because this belongs to the active snapshot/control schema tasks; reported the blocker in team chat after verifying event-specific tests passed.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck -- --pretty false` failed after broadening provider control values with TS2322/TS2345/TS2379/TS1361 errors in delegationChildRuntime.ts, providers.ts, providerSnapshots.ts, and providerConformance.verification.ts.
bugs.root_cause: The provider control value union widened selected control/default/option values beyond strings, but a few helpers still accepted string-only option types or returned generic selected control values into legacy string fields.
bugs.fix: Added a widened AnyProviderControlOption helper type, moved providerControlOptionValue to a value import, cast manifest control options at generic snapshot/permission boundaries, and narrowed legacy selected model/effort reads to strings.

bugs.error_message: `cargo fmt --check` failed because `src-tauri/src/providers/snapshots.rs` import ordering did not match rustfmt after provider snapshot control value types were added.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent control-kind edits changed the snapshot imports after the Capabilities patch had already formatted the file.
bugs.fix: Ran targeted `rustfmt --edition 2021 src-tauri/src/providers/snapshots.rs`; reran `cargo fmt --check`, `git diff --check`, snapshot/registry tests, and clippy successfully.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with TS2339 at `src/lib/providerConformance.verification.ts(537,18)`: `Property 'reason' does not exist on type 'ProviderHydrateResult'`.
bugs.root_cause: Concurrent Conformance fixture edits read `reason` from the hydrate result union without first narrowing away the successful `messages` variant.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by LegacyAudit because the file is in Conformance's active edit scope; reported the blocker in team chat and left the file untouched.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with TS2339 at `src/lib/providerConformance.verification.ts(537,18)`: `Property 'reason' does not exist on type 'ProviderHydrateResult'`.
bugs.root_cause: The unsupported hydrate fixture asserted the `status` with a helper that does not narrow the `ProviderHydrateResult` discriminated union before reading `reason`.
bugs.fix: Changed the hydrate status check to an assertion on `hydrate.status === "unsupported"`, which narrows the union before the reason assertion.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with TS2339 at `src/lib/anthropicProviderEvents.verification.ts(264,29)`: `Property 'message' does not exist on type 'NormalizedProviderEvent'`.
bugs.root_cause: A concurrent Claude event verification fixture reads the error-event `message` field without first narrowing the normalized event union to `kind === "error"`.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by Conformance because the file is owned by the concurrent ClaudeEvents task; reported the blocker in team chat.

bugs.error_message: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` failed with clippy::question_mark at `src-tauri/src/providers/claude.rs:589`: `let Some(content) = content else { return None; }` can be `let content = content?;`.
bugs.root_cause: A concurrent Claude ProviderRuntimeEvent mapper change introduced a let-else early return pattern that violates the repository's denied Clippy question_mark lint.
bugs.fix: Not fixed by DescriptorDrift because the code path is owned by the concurrent ClaudeEvents task; reported the blocker in team chat.

bugs.error_message: `npm run typecheck` failed with TS2339 at `src/lib/anthropicProviderEvents.verification.ts(264,29)`: `Property 'message' does not exist on type 'NormalizedProviderEvent'`.
bugs.root_cause: The Anthropic runtime error fixture used an equality helper on `message.event.kind`; that helper checks the value but does not narrow the `NormalizedProviderEvent` union.
bugs.fix: Replaced the equality helper with an assertion on `message.event.kind === "error"` before reading the error message field.

bugs.error_message: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` failed with clippy::question_mark at `src-tauri/src/providers/claude.rs:589`: `let Some(content) = content else { return None; }` can be `let content = content?;`.
bugs.root_cause: The Claude ProviderRuntimeEvent mapper used let-else to return `None` from an `Option`-returning function, which triggers the repository's denied `question_mark` lint.
bugs.fix: Changed the assistant content extraction to use `and_then(Value::as_array)?` before building the runtime content envelope.

bugs.error_message: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` failed with clippy::unwrap_used in new Codex ProviderRuntimeEvent tests at `src-tauri/src/providers/codex.rs`.
bugs.root_cause: The new Rust tests used `into_iter().next().unwrap()` after asserting vector length, but the repository denies `unwrap_used` in tests too.
bugs.fix: Replaced the unwrap-based extraction with indexed clones after the explicit length assertions.

bugs.error_message: User reopened a rosync widget Codex session with empty chat history and sending failed with `Codex app-server thread/resume failed code=-32600: no rollout found for thread id 898626b3-d01d-45c0-a854-799485428a23`.
bugs.root_cause: Persisted OpenAI runtime metadata could keep a Codex thread id after the corresponding rollout JSONL disappeared. T64 treated any resume id as resumable, so app-server `thread/resume` failed instead of starting a fresh thread.
bugs.fix: Codex app-server sends now preflight the stored thread id against local rollout files and start a fresh app-server thread when the rollout is missing. Codex history hydrate also reports `codex_rollout_missing`, and the OpenAI runtime clears the stale resume id when that reason is returned.

bugs.error_message: Provider thinking/prompts may not cancel on Windows because provider CLIs run through `cmd /C` shims and direct `Child::kill()` only targets the wrapper process.
bugs.root_cause: The provider adapters used direct `child.kill()`/`child.wait()` on the process returned by Rust. On Windows that process can be `cmd.exe` from the npm shim wrapper, leaving the actual provider CLI child process alive.
bugs.fix: Added provider utility `terminate_child_process`, which uses `taskkill /PID <pid> /T /F` with `CREATE_NO_WINDOW` on Windows before falling back to `Child::kill()` and `wait()`, then routed Claude, Codex, and Cursor cancel/close/replacement cleanup through it.

bugs.error_message: The T64 MCP inserter adds Terminal 64 MCP but wipes/hides other installed MCPs such as Roblox Studio in provider MCP JSON configs.
bugs.root_cause: Generated Claude MCP configs were Terminal-64-only, so passing them to Claude could hide existing user/project MCP servers for that run. Frontend delegation helpers also replaced the entire `terminal-64` env object instead of only updating Terminal 64 delegation keys, and malformed existing MCP JSON could be treated as an empty config.
bugs.fix: Added shared MCP JSON merge helpers, made generated Claude delegation/approver configs merge existing user and project MCP servers before replacing only Terminal 64 entries, kept Cursor/Claude project insertion on the same preserve-siblings helper, made frontend delegation env updates preserve unrelated env fields, and made malformed existing MCP JSON fail closed instead of being overwritten.
